title: Gaming Data Engineer
full_description: <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><strong>Who is Blueprint?</strong>&nbsp;</span></p> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">We are a technology solutions firm headquartered in Bellevue, Washington, with a strong presence across the United States. Unified by a shared passion for solving complicated problems, our people are our greatest asset. We use technology as a tool to bridge the gap between strategy and execution, powered by the knowledge, skills, and the&nbsp;expertise&nbsp;of our teams, who all have unique perspectives and years of experience across multiple industries.&nbsp;We’re&nbsp;bold, smart, agile, and fun.&nbsp;</span></p> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><strong>What does Blueprint do?</strong>&nbsp;</span></p> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Blueprint helps organizations unlock value from existing assets by&nbsp;leveraging&nbsp;cutting-edge&nbsp;technology to create&nbsp;additional&nbsp;revenue streams and new lines of business. We connect strategy, business solutions, products, and services to transform and grow companies.&nbsp;</span></p> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><strong>Why Blueprint?</strong>&nbsp;</span></p> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">At Blueprint, we believe in the power of possibility and are passionate about bringing it to life. Whether you join our bustling product division, our multifaceted services team or you want to grow your career in human resources, your ability to make an impact is amplified when you join one of our teams. You’ll focus on solving unique business problems while gaining hands-on experience with the world’s best technology. We believe in unique perspectives and build teams of people with diverse skillsets and backgrounds. At Blueprint, you’ll have the opportunity to work with multiple clients and teams, such as data science and product development, all while learning, growing, and developing new solutions. We guarantee you won’t find a better place to work and thrive than at Blueprint.&nbsp;</span></p> <div> <h2><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"></span></h2> <div> <h2><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">In This Role</span></h2> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">We are seeking a Data Engineer / Analytics Engineer to support the collection, integration, and analysis of operational technology (OT) and infrastructure data across large-scale, mission-critical facilities. This role will focus on extracting data from building management, automation, monitoring, and other vendor platforms, transforming and normalizing data into standardized schemas, and integrating it into enterprise analytics and telemetry platforms. The ideal candidate will have strong experience with ETL processes, data engineering, Kusto, SQL, Microsoft analytics technologies, and automation tools, with the ability to develop scalable data pipelines, troubleshoot data collection challenges, and collaborate with cross-functional stakeholders to improve data visibility, compliance tracking, and operational reporting.</span></p> <h2><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Responsibilities</span></h2> <ul> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Design, develop, and maintain data architectures and schemas to support data ingestion from multiple operational and infrastructure systems.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Develop and maintain automated ETL/ELT processes for extracting, transforming, and loading data from diverse data sources.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Build and support API integrations, database queries, scripting solutions, and file-parsing methods to acquire device inventory and configuration-state data.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Normalize and standardize data across various platforms and vendor applications into a common data model.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Integrate data into enterprise telemetry, monitoring, reporting, and analytics platforms.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Design and optimize data pipelines to support compliance monitoring, change tracking, and operational analytics.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Implement and maintain moderately complex logical and physical database designs.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Create and maintain dashboards, reports, and data visualizations using real-world operational datasets.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Troubleshoot and resolve production data collection, integration, and quality issues.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Develop technical documentation, data schemas, interface specifications, and telemetry requirements.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Collaborate with internal stakeholders, subject matter experts, and external vendors to gather requirements and support project delivery.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Participate in project planning, design discussions, and weekly status reviews to track progress, risks, and blockers.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Support ongoing enhancements as new systems, applications, and data requirements are introduced.</span></li> </ul> <h2><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Required Qualifications</span></h2> <ul> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Engineering, or a related technical field.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">3–5 years of experience in Data Engineering, Analytics Engineering, Business Intelligence, or a related field.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">3+ years of experience with Microsoft analytics and data platform technologies.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Strong understanding of relational database concepts, data modeling, and database design.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Experience building and supporting automated data flows and telemetry pipelines.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Experience developing moderately complex data visualizations and reporting solutions.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Experience working with APIs, scripting, database queries, and structured data formats.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Strong problem-solving, troubleshooting, and analytical skills.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Excellent communication skills with the ability to work independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced environment.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Ability to manage multiple priorities and meet project deadlines.</span></li> </ul> <h2><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; title: Senior AI Data Engineer – Telemetry Reporting & Analytics
full_description: <p><strong>Who is Blueprint?</strong>&nbsp;</p> <p>We are a technology solutions firm headquartered in Bellevue, Washington, with a strong presence across the United States. Unified by a shared passion for solving complicated problems, our people are our greatest asset. We use technology as a tool to bridge the gap between strategy and execution, powered by the knowledge, skills, and the&nbsp;expertise&nbsp;of our teams, who all have unique perspectives and years of experience across multiple industries.&nbsp;We’re&nbsp;bold, smart, agile, and fun.&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>What does Blueprint do?</strong>&nbsp;</p> <p>Blueprint helps organizations unlock value from existing assets by&nbsp;leveraging&nbsp;cutting-edge&nbsp;technology to create&nbsp;additional&nbsp;revenue streams and new lines of business. We connect strategy, business solutions, products, and services to transform and grow companies.&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Why Blueprint?</strong>&nbsp;</p> <p>At Blueprint, we believe in the power of possibility and are passionate about bringing it to life.&nbsp;Whether you join our bustling product division, our multifaceted services team or you want to grow your career in human resources, your ability to make an impact is amplified when you join one of our teams.&nbsp;You’ll&nbsp;focus on solving unique business problems while gaining hands-on experience with the world’s best technology. We believe in unique perspectives and build teams of people with diverse skillsets and backgrounds.&nbsp;At Blueprint, you’ll have the opportunity to work with multiple clients and teams, such as data science and product development, all while learning, growing, and developing new solutions.&nbsp;We guarantee you&nbsp;won’t&nbsp;find a better place to work and thrive than at Blueprint.&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>In This Role</strong></p> <p>In this role, you will design, develop, and support telemetry reporting, analytics, automation, and AI-assisted solutions that improve visibility into service health, availability, operational performance, telemetry coverage, and data quality. You will build dashboards, reports, data models, automation workflows, and analytics components that help engineering and operations teams monitor production systems, identify issues, and make data-driven decisions.</p> <p>The role includes developing Power BI reports, Grafana dashboards, Kusto-based analytics, SQL queries, ETL/data pipelines, and AI-enabled reporting components. You will also validate production solutions, troubleshoot reporting and data issues, support scheduled refreshes, maintain documentation, and collaborate with cross-functional teams to deliver reliable and scalable analytics solutions.</p> <p><strong>Responsibilities</strong></p> <ul> <li>Design, develop, enhance, validate, maintain, and support telemetry reporting, analytics, automation, and AI-assisted solutions.</li> <li>Build and maintain Power BI reports, Grafana dashboards, semantic models, and analytics solutions that deliver actionable business and operational insights.</li> <li>Develop, optimize, and maintain Azure Data Explorer/Kusto queries, functions, data models, scheduled tables, materialized tables, snapshot tables, and supporting datasets.</li> <li>Integrate enterprise data sources and develop SQL queries, ETL processes, and data pipelines needed for reporting and analytics solutions.</li> <li>Create automation solutions using Python, PowerShell, C#, or similar scripting/programming languages to streamline operational tasks and business processes.</li> <li>Develop AI-enabled reporting and analytics components, including agent-based workflows, prompt assets, knowledge files, and automated reporting capabilities.</li> <li>Validate data quality, business logic, report accuracy, dashboard performance, and scheduled refreshes to ensure reliable production solutions.</li> <li>Monitor, troubleshoot, and resolve data pipeline, query, reporting, dashboard, and production issues.</li> <li>Create and maintain technical documentation, reusable components, and engineering best practices to support long-term maintainability.</li> <li>Collaborate with engineering, operations, and business stakeholders to deliver scalable, high-quality analytics and reporting solutions.</li> <li>Support continuous improvement of telemetry visibility, telemetry availability, clean telemetry, and telemetry data quality.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Qualifications</strong></p> <ul> <li>5+ years of experience in reporting and analytics, including Power BI, data modeling, DAX, Grafana or similar reporting platforms, and web-based reporting.</li> <li>5+ years of experience in data engineering, including SQL, ETL/data pipelines, data modeling, and cloud-based analytics platforms.</li> <li>3+ years of experience with automation, scripting, or application development using Python, PowerShell, C#, or similar languages.</li> <li>Hands-on experience developing dashboards, reports, analytics models, and operational reporting solutions.</li> <li>Experience building and optimizing Kusto/ADX queries or working with similar large-scale analytics/query platforms.</li> <li>Experience integrating enterprise data sources and supporting data pipelines for analytics and reporting use cases.</li> <li>Experience validating report accuracy, business logic, data quality, and production refresh processes.</li> <li>Ability to troubleshoot production issues related to data pipelines, queries, dashboards, reporting solutions, and automation workflows.</li> <li>Experience working with Azure DevOps or similar work item tracking and delivery tools.</li> <li>Strong documentation, collaboration, and communication skills.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Preferred Qualifications</strong></p> <ul> <li>Experience with telemetry, service health, availability monitoring, operational dashboards, or production monitoring systems.</li> <li>Experience supporting enterprise-scale reporting, analytics, or data engineering environments.</li> <li>Experience with AI-assisted reporting, agentic solutions, Copilot-style agents, prompt assets, or automated report generation.</li> <li>Experience developing reusable analytics components, reporting templates, automation frameworks, or shared data models.</li> <li>Experience working across the full solution lifecycle, including requirements gathering, development, validation, production support, and continuous enhancement.</li> <li>Experience improving telemetry coverage, telemetry data quality, operational visibility, and production reliability.</li> <li>Familiarity with modern cloud analytics, observability, and data platform technologies.</li> <li>Ability to deliver high-quality work with minimal rework and strong first-pass acceptance during stakeholder review.</li> <li>Ability to work effectively in a hybrid, cross-functional engineering environment.</li> </ul> <p><strong>&nbsp;</strong></p> <p><strong>Salary Range</strong>&nbsp;</p> <p>At Blueprint, we strive to offer competitive pay that reflects the value of our team members. Compensation for this role is influenced by a variety of factors, including skills, education, responsibilities, experience, and geographic market. For candidates based in Washington State, the anticipated salary range is&nbsp;<strong>$150,000– $170,000 per annum. &nbsp;</strong>Please note that we typically do not hire new employees at the top of the posted range. Actual starting pay will be determined based on experience, skills, and internal equity. The final salary and job title may vary depending on the selected candidate’s qualifications and could fall outside the stated range.</p> <p><strong>Equal Opportunity Employer</strong>&nbsp;</p> <p>Blueprint Technologies, LLC is an equal employment opportunity employer. Qualified applicants are considered without regard to race, color, age, disability, sex, gender identity or expression, orientation, veteran/military status, religion, national origin, ancestry, marital, or familial status, genetic information, citizenship, or any other status protected by law.&nbsp;</p> <p>If you need&nbsp;assistance&nbsp;or a reasonable accommodation to complete the application process, please reach out to: <a href="mailto:recruiting@bpcs.com">recruiting@bpcs.com</a>&nbsp;</p> <p>Blueprint believe in the importance of a healthy and happy team, which is why our comprehensive benefits package includes:&nbsp;</p> <ul> <li>Medical, dental, and vision coverage&nbsp;</li> <li>Flexible Spending Account&nbsp;</li> <li>401k program&nbsp;</li> <li>Competitive PTO offerings&nbsp;</li> <li>Parental Leave&nbsp;</li> <li>Opportunities for professional growth and development&nbsp;</li> </ul> <p><strong>Location:</strong> Hybrid</p> <p>&nbsp;</p>

title: Senior Software Engineer, Data Engineering
full_description: <p>At Rockstar Games, we create world-class entertainment experiences.&nbsp;</p> <p>Become part of a team working on some of the most rewarding, large-scale creative projects to be found in any entertainment medium - all within an inclusive, highly-motivated environment where you can learn and collaborate with some of the most talented people in the industry.</p> <p>Rockstar Games is seeking a Senior Software Engineer in Data Engineering to join a team focused on building a cutting-edge game analytics platform and tools to better understand our players and enhance their experience in our games. &nbsp;&nbsp;</p> <p>The ideal candidate will be skilled in developing complex ingestion and transformation processes with an emphasis on reliability and performance. In collaboration with Data Engineers, Machine Learning Engineers, and other Software Engineers, the candidate will empower the team of Analysts and Data Scientists to deliver data driven insights and applications to company stakeholders. &nbsp;</p> <p>This is a full-time, in-office position based out of Rockstar’s NYC headquarters in Downtown Manhattan.&nbsp;</p> <h4><strong>WHAT WE DO</strong></h4> <ul> <li>The Rockstar Games Online Services team builds the technology foundation that powers our games and delivers world-class player experiences. &nbsp;</li> <li>Our Data Engineering group manages petabyte-scale data, integrating dozens of streaming and batch sources with strict requirements for reliability, compliance, and low-latency processing.&nbsp;</li> <li>We approach data engineering with the rigor of software engineering by applying modern practices such as clean architecture, modular design, and automated testing. Our focus is on building scalable, reusable platforms and frameworks that enable our partners in Data Science and Game Development to deliver insights and unlock new possibilities for players.&nbsp;</li> </ul> <h4><strong>RESPONSIBILITIES</strong></h4> <ul> <li>Design, build, and maintain high-throughput streaming and batch data processing services, with a primary focus on raw and bronze-level ingestion.&nbsp;</li> <li>Develop and operate stream–based applications responsible for real-time data transformation, enrichment, validation, and routing.&nbsp;</li> <li>Own and evolve event schemas and data contracts, including Avro schemas and Schema Registry governance.&nbsp;</li> <li>Ensure scalability, fault tolerance, and performance of streaming and ingestion pipelines under heavy load.&nbsp;</li> <li>Contribute to platform-level concerns such as deployment automation, observability, operational tooling, and CI/CD.&nbsp;</li> <li>Participate in the design and implementation of cloud-native data infrastructure supporting real-time and batch workloads.&nbsp;</li> </ul> <h4><strong>REQUIREMENTS</strong></h4> <ul> <li>5+ years of professional experience building production software systems, preferably in a distributed or data-intensive environment.&nbsp;</li> <li>Strong experience with Java (and/or Scala) as well as Python, in backend or data processing applications.&nbsp;</li> <li>Experience designing and operating streaming systems using Kafka or Kafka Streams (or similar).&nbsp;</li> <li>Experience working with event-driven architectures, including schema evolution and compatibility.&nbsp;</li> <li>Experience building real-time and/or near-real-time data pipelines at scale.&nbsp;</li> <li>Solid understanding of distributed systems concepts (partitioning, fault tolerance, backpressure, exactly-once/at-least-once semantics).&nbsp;</li> <li>Familiarity with Avro, Protobuf, or similar serialization formats and schema governance practices.&nbsp;</li> </ul> <h4><strong>PLUSES</strong></h4> <p>Please note that these are desirable skills and are not required to apply for the position.</p> <ul> <li>Experience with Databricks, particularly for ingestion, bronze-layer processing, or structured streaming.&nbsp;</li> <li>Experience deploying and scaling applications in containerized environments (e.g., Kubernetes, AKS).&nbsp;</li> <li>Experience working with artifact repositories (e.g., Artifactory, ProGet, Maven repositories).&nbsp;</li> <li>Experience with Infrastructure-as-Code (e.g., Terraform, Databricks Asset Bundles).&nbsp;</li> <li>Familiarity with the Microsoft Azure cloud ecosystem.&nbsp;</li> <li>Familiarity with Apache Spark.&nbsp;</li> <li>Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines, automated testing, and deployment workflows.&nbsp;</li> </ul> <h4><strong>HOW TO APPLY</strong></h4> <p>Please apply with a resume and cover letter demonstrating how you meet the skills above. If we would like to move forward with your application, a Rockstar recruiter will reach out to you to explain next steps and guide you through the process.</p> <p>Rockstar is committed to creating a work environment that promotes equal opportunity, dignity and respect. In line with this commitment, Rockstar will provide reasonable accommodations to qualified job applicants with disabilities during the recruitment process in order for such applicants to be considered for the position for which they are applying, as well as to qualified employees to enable them to perform the essential functions of their roles. title: Senior Software Engineer, Data Engineering
full_description: <p>At Rockstar Games, we create world-class entertainment experiences.&nbsp;</p> <p>Become part of a team working on some of the most rewarding, large-scale creative projects to be found in any entertainment medium - all within an inclusive, highly-motivated environment where you can learn and collaborate with some of the most talented people in the industry.</p> <p>Rockstar Games is seeking a Senior Software Engineer in Data Engineering to join a team focused on building a cutting-edge game analytics platform and tools to better understand our players and enhance their experience in our games. &nbsp;&nbsp;</p> <p>The ideal candidate will be skilled in developing complex ingestion and transformation processes with an emphasis on reliability and performance. In collaboration with Data Engineers, Machine Learning Engineers, and other Software Engineers, the candidate will empower the team of Analysts and Data Scientists to deliver data driven insights and applications to company stakeholders. &nbsp;</p> <p>This is a full-time, in-office position based out of Rockstar’s new state-of-the-art game development studio in Carlsbad, CA.</p> <h4><strong>WHAT WE DO</strong></h4> <ul> <li>The Rockstar Games Online Services team builds the technology foundation that powers our games and delivers world-class player experiences. &nbsp;</li> <li>Our Data Engineering group manages petabyte-scale data, integrating dozens of streaming and batch sources with strict requirements for reliability, compliance, and low-latency processing.&nbsp;</li> <li>We approach data engineering with the rigor of software engineering by applying modern practices such as clean architecture, modular design, and automated testing. Our focus is on building scalable, reusable platforms and frameworks that enable our partners in Data Science and Game Development to deliver insights and unlock new possibilities for players.&nbsp;</li> </ul> <h4><strong>RESPONSIBILITIES</strong></h4> <ul> <li>Design, build, and maintain high-throughput streaming and batch data processing services, with a primary focus on raw and bronze-level ingestion.&nbsp;</li> <li>Develop and operate stream–based applications responsible for real-time data transformation, enrichment, validation, and routing.&nbsp;</li> <li>Own and evolve event schemas and data contracts, including Avro schemas and Schema Registry governance.&nbsp;</li> <li>Ensure scalability, fault tolerance, and performance of streaming and ingestion pipelines under heavy load.&nbsp;</li> <li>Contribute to platform-level concerns such as deployment automation, observability, operational tooling, and CI/CD.&nbsp;</li> <li>Participate in the design and implementation of cloud-native data infrastructure supporting real-time and batch workloads.&nbsp;</li> </ul> <h4><strong>REQUIREMENTS</strong></h4> <ul> <li>5+ years of professional experience building production software systems, preferably in a distributed or data-intensive environment.&nbsp;</li> <li>Strong experience with Java (and/or Scala) as well as Python, in backend or data processing applications.&nbsp;</li> <li>Experience designing and operating streaming systems using Kafka or Kafka Streams (or similar).&nbsp;</li> <li>Experience working with event-driven architectures, including schema evolution and compatibility.&nbsp;</li> <li>Experience building real-time and/or near-real-time data pipelines at scale.&nbsp;</li> <li>Solid understanding of distributed systems concepts (partitioning, fault tolerance, backpressure, exactly-once/at-least-once semantics).&nbsp;</li> <li>Familiarity with Avro, Protobuf, or similar serialization formats and schema governance practices.&nbsp;</li> </ul> <h4><strong>PLUSES</strong></h4> <p>Please note that these are desirable skills and are not required to apply for the position.</p> <ul> <li>Experience with Databricks, particularly for ingestion, bronze-layer processing, or structured streaming.&nbsp;</li> <li>Experience deploying and scaling applications in containerized environments (e.g., Kubernetes, AKS).&nbsp;</li> <li>Experience working with artifact repositories (e.g., Artifactory, ProGet, Maven repositories).&nbsp;</li> <li>Experience with Infrastructure-as-Code (e.g., Terraform, Databricks Asset Bundles).&nbsp;</li> <li>Familiarity with the Microsoft Azure cloud ecosystem.&nbsp;</li> <li>Familiarity with Apache Spark.&nbsp;</li> <li>Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines, automated testing, and deployment workflows.&nbsp;</li> </ul> <h4><strong>HOW TO APPLY</strong></h4> <p>Please apply with a resume and cover letter demonstrating how you meet the skills above. If we would like to move forward with your application, a Rockstar recruiter will reach out to you to explain next steps and guide you through the process.</p> <p>Rockstar is committed to creating a work environment that promotes equal opportunity, dignity and respect. In line with this commitment, Rockstar will provide reasonable accommodations to qualified job applicants with disabilities during the recruitment process in order for such applicants to be considered for the position for which they are applying, as well as to qualified employees to enable them to perform the essential functions of their roles. If you need more information about Rockstar’s reasonable accommodation policies or process, or need to request an accommodation, please notify your recruiter during the interview process.&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp;</p> <p>If you’ve got the right skills for the job, we want to hear from you. We encourage applications from all suitable candidates regardless of age, disability, gender identity, sexual orientation, religion, belief, race, or any other protected category.</p> <p><span style="color: rgb(0, 0, 0); font-size: 8pt;">#LI-BB1</span></p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p>The pay range for this position in San Diego at the start of employment is expected to be between the range below* per year.&nbsp;However, base pay offered is based on market location, and may vary further depending on individualized factors for job candidates, such as job-related knowledge, skills, experience, and other objective business considerations. &nbsp;</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>Subject to those same considerations, the total compensation package for this position may also include other elements, including a bonus and/or equity awards, in addition to a full range of medical, financial, and/or other benefits. &nbsp;Details of participation in these benefit plans will be provided if an employee receives an offer of employment. If hired, employee will be in an "at-will position" and the company reserves the right to modify base salary (as well as any other discretionary payment or compensation or benefit program) at any time, including for reasons related to individual performance, company or individual department/team performance, and market factors.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p></div><div class="title">*San Diego Base Pay Range</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$121,400</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$161,800 USD</span></div></div></div>

title: Data Engineer
full_description: <p>Working at CreateFuture<br><br><a href="https://createfuture.com/">CreateFuture</a> is an AI-native consulting partner where people do work that matters and are supported to do it well. We work alongside organisations such as PayPal, adidas, NatWest, FanDuel and Money Saving Expert, building digital products and services that make a difference&nbsp; while always putting people first.</p> <p>We’re a team of creators. We write code, shape delivery, build go-to-market strategies, develop AI solutions and create the practices that support our people. We work side by side with our clients, challenging what’s not working and helping them to build the future. Our commitment to craft, quality, and culture has helped us scale to over 600 people in just a few years.</p> <p>Our UK Benefits&nbsp;</p> <ul> <li> <p>35 days leave (including bank holidays).&nbsp;</p> </li> <li> <p>Private medical insurance.</p> </li> <li> <p>Enhanced parental and adoption leave.&nbsp;</p> </li> <li> <p>Financial coaching + 5% pension match.</p> </li> <li> <p>40 hours of paid learning and development.</p> </li> </ul> <p><a href="https://drive.google.com/file/d/1-uZ_KOuW1SI-xoZ0Sd0WJCisULzkYox4/view?usp=sharing">&nbsp;View our full list of UK benefits.&nbsp; <br><br></a>CreateFuture is a<a href="https://www.greatplacetowork.co.uk/certified-company/1572759"> Great Place to Work-Certified™ company</a> and has won Best Workplaces UK multiple years in a row.</p> <p>Join us on our journey. Let’s create tomorrow, together, today.<br><br></p> <h3>About the role and team:</h3> <p>Role: Data Engineer&nbsp;</p> <p>Hands-on delivery engineers working under the Architect to execute the migration. Responsible for building and fixing data pipelines, resolving technical debt, and getting the platform to a stable, production-ready state.</p> <h3><strong>What you’ll be doing:</strong></h3> <ul> <li> <p>Design, build, and optimise data pipelines on the AWS stack</p> </li> <li> <p>Identify and resolve existing technical debt in the migration</p> </li> <li> <p>Work collaboratively with the client's engineering team to transfer knowledge</p> </li> <li> <p>Contribute to stabilising the platform and hitting delivery milestones</p> </li> <li> <p>Support the Architect in defining and implementing the target state</p> </li> </ul> <h3><strong>We’d love to talk to you if you:&nbsp;&nbsp;</strong></h3> <ul> <li> <p>AWS data stack: S3, Glue, Redshift, Lake Formation</p> </li> <li> <p>Lakehouse patterns and architecture</p> </li> <li> <p>Python and SQL — strong proficiency</p> </li> <li> <p>Experience in regulated or high-volume data environments</p> </li> <li> <p>Betting and gaming domain knowledge <strong>required</strong></p> </li> </ul> <p>This role is Hybrid and would require 2-3 days onsite in Manchester</p> <p>&nbsp;</p><div class="content-conclusion"><h3 id="What-we’ll-offer-you:" data-renderer-start-pos="8044"><strong data-renderer-mark="true">What we’ll offer you:</strong></h3> <p>We trust people to do their best work. That means flexibility over rigid rules, impact over activity, and real investment in your growth both professionally and personally. You’ll be part of a supportive, and friendly culture, surrounded by smart, curious people who care deeply about what they do.<br><br>We offer flexible working, including hybrid and remote options. Our office hubs are located in Edinburgh, Leeds, Manchester, London and Bulgaria, with occasional travel to client sites or CreateFuture offices when needed.</p> <p>We trust you to manage your time balancing collaboration with client time and focused work. What matters is the impact you have, not how busy you look.</p> <h3><strong>Our hiring process</strong></h3> <p>We try to keep our hiring process clear, fair and respectful of your time. We aim to get back to everyone who applies and we will be upfront about where you are in the process.</p> <p>It usually looks like this:</p> <ul> <li>Call with our Talent Acquisition Team&nbsp;</li> <li>Role specific capability interview&nbsp;</li> </ul> <p>Depending on the role, we might also ask you to do a short presentation, a practical or technical task or have a values focused conversation. We will explain what is involved before anything happens.</p> <h3><strong>Inclusion at CreateFuture&nbsp;</strong></h3> <p>We believe diverse teams build better workplaces and better products. We want CreateFuture to be a place where people feel able to be themselves and do their best work.</p> <p>If you need any adjustments or support during the application process, just. We will do what we can to help.</p> <p>We look forward to your application!</p></div>

title: Lead Data Engineer
full_description: <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>Who we are</strong></span></p> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;">CreateFuture is fast becoming the UK’s most recognisable digital consultancy, with years of experience building digital products and services for major organisations whilst putting our people first. We have offices in the centre of Edinburgh, Leeds, Manchester, and London as well as remote employees located throughout the country.&nbsp;</span></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;">We are a team of creators - whether that’s code, project plans, go to market strategies, culture initiatives, marketing campaigns, large language models or people policies. And together, with our clients, we create the future. This has seen us collaborate and partner across a multitude of industries and sectors, with the likes of PayPal, adidas, Natwest, FanDuel and Money Saving Expert, to name just a few.</span></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Our reputation as a partner determined to deliver high-quality, robust and thoughtful products has enabled us to scale to over 500 people in the last couple of years, and it is our amazing people - along with the safe, supportive and friendly culture we have built - that makes CreateFuture a great place to work. Don’t just take our word for it though, we have been recognised by Best Workplaces UK multiple years in a row - across a number of categories - and our employee exit rate is astonishingly low.&nbsp;</span></p> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>Join us on our journey… Let’s create something awesome, together, today.</strong></span></p> <p><br><br></p> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>About the role and team:</strong></span></p> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;">We're looking for an experienced Lead Data Engineer to join our growing Data Practice, specialising in iGaming. You'll work as part of a multidisciplinary team delivering data solutions for some of the world's largest betting and gaming operators — organisations processing millions of events per day across sportsbook, casino, and exchange platforms.</span></p> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;">This is a role for someone who understands the pressures of the iGaming data landscape: sub-second odds feeds, high-stakes data integrity requirements, complex regulatory reporting, and large-scale platform migrations from legacy on-premises estates to cloud-native architectures on AWS.</span></p> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;">As a Lead, you'll shape technical direction, mentor engineers, and act as a trusted partner to client data and engineering teams.</span></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <h1><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>What you'll be doing</strong></span></h1> <ul> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Leading large-scale data migrations from on-premises or legacy platforms to cloud-native architectures on AWS — including Lakehouse designs using Databricks or Snowflake</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Designing and building low-latency data pipelines that process real-time betting events, odds updates, and market data at high throughput</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Implementing robust data integrity controls — validation, reconciliation, and auditability — across betting, trading, and customer data domains</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Architecting streaming and micro-batch solutions using AWS Kinesis, Apache Kafka, or MSK for real-time sportsbook and in-play data feeds</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Working with AWS platform services including S3, Glue, Lambda, Athena, Redshift, Lake Formation, Step Functions, and MWAA</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Supporting regulatory and compliance reporting — ensuring data pipelines meet the auditability and lineage requirements of the UK Gambling Commission and international regulators</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Leading and mentoring a team of data engineers, setting standards for code quality, testing, and documentation</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Contributing to technical proposals, pre-sales scoping, and architecture reviews with iGaming clients</span></li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <h1><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>We'd love to talk to you if...</strong></span></h1> <h2><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>You know the iGaming data landscape</strong></span></h2> <ul> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">You've worked in or closely with sportsbook, exchange, or casino platforms — and understand the data models behind markets, selections, bets, and settlements</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">You've built or maintained low-latency data feeds — odds ingestion, in-play event streams, price change feeds — where milliseconds matter</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">You understand the data integrity challenges specific to betting: reconciliation between trading and settlement systems, detection of data drift, and auditability for regulatory purposes</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">You've delivered or contributed to large-scale data migrations — moving high-volume transactional or event data from legacy estates to the cloud without losing fidelity or uptime</span></li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <h2><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>You're strong on AWS data engineering</strong></span></h2> <ul> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">AWS is your primary cloud — you're confident across Glue, Lambda, Athena, S3, Redshift, RDS, Kinesis, MSK, and Step Functions</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">You've built real-time or near-real-time pipelines using streaming frameworks — Kinesis Data Streams, Kafka, Flink, or Spark Streaming</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">You understand AWS cost models and how to optimise high-throughput workloads — Spot compute, reserved capacity, and right-sizing for event-driven architectures</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">AWS certifications are a bonus — Solutions Architect or Data Analytics Specialty</span></li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <h2><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>You know your tools</strong></span></h2> <ul> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Solid experience with Python, SQL, and Spark — and pipeline tools such as dbt or Airflow</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Comfortable with data quality frameworks and testing — Great Expectations, dbt tests, or custom validation layers</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Experience with infrastructure-as-code (Terraform, AWS CDK) is a plus</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Familiar with broader cloud platforms — Azure (Data Factory, Synapse) or GCP (Dataflow, BigQuery) — is beneficial but not essential</span></li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <h2><span title: Principal Data Engineer
full_description: <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>Who we are</strong></span></p> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;">CreateFuture is fast becoming the UK’s most recognisable digital consultancy, with years of experience building digital products and services for major organisations whilst putting our people first. We have offices in the centre of Edinburgh, Leeds, Manchester, and London as well as remote employees located throughout the country.&nbsp;</span></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;">We are a team of creators - whether that’s code, project plans, go to market strategies, culture initiatives, marketing campaigns, large language models or people policies. And together, with our clients, we create the future. This has seen us collaborate and partner across a multitude of industries and sectors, with the likes of PayPal, adidas, Natwest, FanDuel and Money Saving Expert, to name just a few.</span></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Our reputation as a partner determined to deliver high-quality, robust and thoughtful products has enabled us to scale to over 500 people in the last couple of years, and it is our amazing people - along with the safe, supportive and friendly culture we have built - that makes CreateFuture a great place to work. Don’t just take our word for it though, we have been recognised by Best Workplaces UK multiple years in a row - across a number of categories - and our employee exit rate is astonishingly low.&nbsp;</span></p> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>Join us on our journey… Let’s create something awesome, together, today.</strong></span></p> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>About the role and team:</strong></span></p> <h1><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>About the role and team</strong></span></h1> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;">We're looking for a Principal Data Engineer to join our Data Practice and own the technical direction of our iGaming data engineering capability. You'll work with some of the world's largest betting and gaming operators — organisations processing hundreds of millions of events per day across sportsbook, casino, exchange, and lottery platforms.</span></p> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;">A Principal at Create Future operates above the delivery line as well as within it. You'll define how we solve the hardest data engineering problems in iGaming: petabyte-scale migrations from legacy on-premises estates, sub-millisecond event pipelines, settlement reconciliation at bookmaker scale, and the data foundations that keep operators compliant with gambling regulators globally.</span></p> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;">You'll influence the shape of engagements from pre-sales through to delivery, grow the engineering talent around you, and be a recognisable voice for Create Future in the iGaming market.</span></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <h1><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>What you'll be doing</strong></span></h1> <h2><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>Technical leadership</strong></span></h2> <ul> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Defining and owning the data engineering architecture patterns Create Future uses across iGaming engagements — migration frameworks, streaming architectures, data integrity standards, and Lakehouse design</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Acting as the most senior technical authority on client engagements — making critical architecture decisions, resolving design disputes, and setting the bar for quality</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Reviewing and signing off technical designs produced by Lead and Senior engineers across active engagements</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Identifying and driving the adoption of new tooling, patterns, and AWS services that improve delivery outcomes across the iGaming practice</span></li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <h2><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>Delivery</strong></span></h2> <ul> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Leading the technical design and execution of large-scale data migrations — moving high-volume transactional and event data from on-premises or legacy cloud estates to AWS, without loss of fidelity or operational continuity</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Architecting real-time and near-real-time data pipelines for sportsbook and in-play use cases — odds feeds, market events, bet placement streams, and settlement pipelines — where latency and correctness are non-negotiable</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Designing and enforcing data integrity frameworks across betting, trading, and customer data domains — including reconciliation between source and target systems, anomaly detection, and audit trail requirements</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Overseeing the implementation of regulatory and compliance reporting pipelines that meet the requirements of the UK Gambling Commission and international licensing bodies</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Working with AWS services including S3, Glue, Lambda, Athena, Redshift, RDS, Kinesis, MSK, Lake Formation, Step Functions, and MWAA — selecting the right tool for each problem rather than defaulting to a stack</span></li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <h2><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>Practice and people</strong></span></h2> <ul> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Mentoring and growing Lead and Senior Data Engineers — through code review, pairing, architecture sessions, and career conversations</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Building the iGaming data engineering playbook: reusable patterns, accelerators, and reference architectures that reduce time-to-value on new engagements</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Contributing to hiring — defining what great looks like for data engineering roles and playing an active part in assessment</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Representing Create Future externally — at industry events, in technical blog posts, and in client-facing settings — as a credible voice on iGaming data engineering</span></li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <h2><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>Commercial</strong></span></h2> <ul> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Shaping and leading technical responses to iGaming opportunities — scoping complex engagements, writing architecture proposals, and presenting to senior client stakeholders</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Identifying expansion opportunities within existing client relationships — spotting data engineering problems we can help solve and framing them as propositions</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Partnering with AWS on joint go-to-market activity where relevant, leveraging Create Future's Advanced Partner status</span></li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <h1><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>What we're looking for</strong></span></h1> <h2><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>Deep iGaming domain expertise</strong></span></h2> <ul> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Extensive experience working in or closely with sportsbook, exchange, or casino platforms — you understand the data models, operational pressures, and regulatory constraints from the inside</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">You've led or made decisive contributions to large-scale data migrations in iGaming — moving live betting, customer, or trading data at scale while maintaining integrity and uptime</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">You've designed or owned low-latency data pipelines where correctness and throughput are both critical — odds ingestion, in-play event streams, bet settlement, or equivalent</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">You understand what data integrity means in a regulated gambling context: reconciliation, audit trails, lineage, and the consequences of getting it wrong</span></li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <h2><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>AWS data engineering mastery</strong></span></h2> <ul> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">AWS is your primary cloud and you can design across the full data engineering stack — from ingestion and streaming through to warehousing, serving, and cost optimisation</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">You have deep experience with streaming architectures — Kinesis, Kafka/MSK, Flink, or Spark Streaming — and know when each is the right choice</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">You can model the cost implications of architecture decisions and advise clients on trade-offs between throughput, latency, durability, and spend</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">AWS certification at professional or specialty level is strongly preferred</span></li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <h2><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>Engineering excellence</strong></span></h2> <ul> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Mastery of Python, SQL, and Spark — and strong opinions on how they should be used in production data pipelines</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Deep experience with dbt, Airflow, or equivalent orchestration — including how to build pipelines that are testable, observable, and operationally sound</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Strong understanding of data quality engineering — not just validation rules, but how to build systems that detect and surface integrity issues before they reach consumers</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Comfortable with infrastructure-as-code (Terraform, AWS CDK) and treating data platform infrastructure as a first-class engineering concern</span></li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <h2><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>Consultancy and leadership</strong></span></h2> <ul> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">You've operated at Principal or equivalent level — making architectural decisions that affect multiple teams, and being accountable for their outcomes</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">You're comfortable in C-suite and VP-level client conversations, translating technical complexity into business risk and opportunity</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">You can hold a room on a difficult technical topic and reach a decision — not just present options</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">You've grown engineering talent around you and take it seriously as a leadership responsibility</span></li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <h1><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>Tech snapshot</strong></span></h1> <table style="width: 76.4826%; height: 439.531px;"> <tbody> <tr style="height: 67.1875px;"> <td style="width: 11.8848%; height: 67.1875px;"> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>Primary cloud</strong></span></p> </td> <td style="width: 84.258%; height: 67.1875px;"> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;">AWS — Glue, Lambda, Athena, S3, Redshift, RDS, Kinesis, MSK, Lake Formation, Step Functions, MWAA</span></p> </td> </tr> <tr style="height: 47.5938px;"> <td style="width: 11.8848%; height: 47.5938px;"> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>Streaming</strong></span></p> </td> <td style="width: 84.258%; height: 47.5938px;"> <p><span title: Data Engineer (Remote, US)
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><h2><strong>About Sayari:&nbsp;</strong></h2> <div> <p>Sayari is the judgment infrastructure for trustworthy AI in economic security and commercial risk. The Sayari Commercial World Model resolves 11.7B+ primary-source records from 250+ jurisdictions forming the ground truth of global commerce. A Judgment Ontology, encoding over a decade of investigative tradecraft, and Superconductor, an agentic orchestration platform, deliver AI that reasons like an expert analyst, shows its work, and traces every finding to its source. Trusted by U.S. Customs and Border Protection, HM Revenue &amp; Customs, and Fortune 500 enterprises, Sayari is used by thousands of professionals across 35+ countries to secure supply chains and dismantle illicit networks. Headquartered in Washington, D.C., with offices in London, Singapore, Tokyo, and Tel Aviv.</p> </div></div><h3><strong>POSITION DESCRIPTION</strong></h3> <p>As a Data Engineer at Sayari, you will be the engine behind the world’s most comprehensive commercial world model. You will join a high-autonomy team responsible for building and scaling the complex orchestration systems that transform billions of primary-source records into actionable intelligence. This is a role for a "builder" who respects the complexity of large-scale ETL and graph databases and is "PhD-curious" about the future of AI-native data products and modern orchestration.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <h4><strong>JOB RESPONSIBILITIES</strong></h4> <ul> <li>Design, build, and maintain scalable data pipelines using Python, Spark, and Airflow to support our core data acquisition and entity resolution engines.</li> </ul> <ul> <li>Collaborate cross-functionally with AI/ML and Product teams to implement new features and AI-native products.</li> <li>Proactively identify and resolve bottlenecks in our complex ETL processes, bringing a fresh perspective to refine and optimize our existing codebase.</li> <li>Contribute to a robust engineering culture through rigorous code reviews, unit testing, and clear communication of design decisions.</li> <li>Own the end-to-end delivery of roadmap tasks within two-week sprints, ensuring work meets high standards for quality, documentation, and performance.</li> <li>Participate in roadmap planning and story refinement, eventually taking ownership of major epics that drive our long-term product defensibility.</li> </ul> <h4>SKILLS &amp; EXPERIENCE</h4> <p><strong>Required</strong></p> <ul> <li>Professional proficiency in Python and experience contributing to shared codebases using Git (branching, PRs, code reviews).</li> <li>3+ years of experience working in Data Engineering&nbsp;</li> <li>Demonstrated experience working with relational databases (PostgreSQL/BigQuery) and an interest in or familiarity with graph databases.</li> <li>Familiarity with distributed computing (Spark) or a strong desire to master it.</li> <li>Strong collaborative skills and the ability to work effectively in an Agile, sprint-based environment.</li> <li>A "self-directed" orientation: ability to move tasks from "assigned" to "complete" with high autonomy and clear communication.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Preferred</strong></p> <ul> <li>Experience with Django, Scala, or Scrapy.</li> <li>Hands-on experience with workflow orchestration tools like Airflow.</li> <li>Experience or strong interest in LLM tuning, deployment, and AI engineering best practices.</li> <li>Experience working with international or non-English datasets.</li> <li>Prior experience working with high-scale, complex data pipelines.</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>The target base salary for this position is $90,000-$120,000 plus company bonus and equity. Final offer amounts are determined by multiple factors including location, local market variances, candidate experience and expertise, internal peer equity, and may vary from the amounts listed above.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <h3><strong>Benefits:&nbsp;</strong></h3> <div> <ul> <li>100% fully paid medical, vision, and dental for employees and their dependents</li> <li>Generous time off; we observe all US federal holidays, close our office for a winter break (12/24-12/31), in addition to granting 18 PTO days and 10 sick days</li> <li>Outstanding compensation package; competitive commissions for revenue roles and bonuses for non-revenue positions</li> <li>A strong commitment to diversity, equity, and inclusion</li> <li>Eligibility to participate in additional benefits such as 401k match up to 5%, 100% paid life insurance (up to $100,000 coverage),, and parental leave</li> <li>A collaborative and positive culture - your team will be as smart and driven as you</li> <li>Limitless growth and learning opportunities</li> </ul> </div> <div>&nbsp;</div> <div><em>Sayari is an equal opportunity employer and strongly encourages diverse candidates to apply. We believe diversity and inclusion mean our team members should reflect the diversity of the United States. No employee or applicant will face discrimination or harassment based on race, color, ethnicity, religion, age, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, disability status, veteran status, genetics, or political affiliation. We strongly encourage applicants of all backgrounds to apply.</em></div><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="title">Pay Range</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$90,000</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$120,000 USD</span></div></div></div>

title: Forward Deployed Engineer, CE (Evergreen)
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><h2><strong>About Sayari:&nbsp;</strong></h2> <div> <p>Sayari is the judgment infrastructure for trustworthy AI in economic security and commercial risk. The Sayari Commercial World Model resolves 11.7B+ primary-source records from 250+ jurisdictions forming the ground truth of global commerce. A Judgment Ontology, encoding over a decade of investigative tradecraft, and Superconductor, an agentic orchestration platform, deliver AI that reasons like an expert analyst, shows its work, and traces every finding to its source. Trusted by U.S. Customs and Border Protection, HM Revenue &amp; Customs, and Fortune 500 enterprises, Sayari is used by thousands of professionals across 35+ countries to secure supply chains and dismantle illicit networks. Headquartered in Washington, D.C., with offices in London, Singapore, Tokyo, and Tel Aviv.</p> </div></div><h3><strong>POSITION DESCRIPTION</strong></h3> <p><strong>At Sayari, we’re always looking for great talent to join our team. This is a proactive hiring post for future Forward Deployed Engineer, CE roles, allowing us to connect with amazing candidates like you before a specific seat opens up.</strong></p> <p>This is not a traditional sales engineering role. As a Forward Deployed Engineer (FDE), you are at the critical intersection of our code, our clients, and their most critical missions. You will be deployed to our most strategic partners, embedding within their environments to solve high-stakes problems that have no textbook answers.</p> <p>You own the "last mile" of our product's impact , ensuring our technology fundamentally transforms how our users confront challenges such as financial crime, supply chain disruption, and threats to national security.</p> <p>You are equal parts engineer, strategist, and operator. One day you might be architecting a data pipeline within a client's cloud environment ; the next, you'll be writing production Python to integrate our API into a proprietary analytics platform ; and by the end of the week, you could be briefing a C-level executive, intelligence analyst, or mission director on the outcomes your work has enabled.</p> <p>You thrive on ambiguity, operate with extreme ownership, and are relentless in your pursuit of delivering real-world value. You will act as a vital conduit between the front lines and our core engineering teams, turning your bespoke solutions into scalable, platform-wide features of Sayari’s product suite.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <h4><strong>JOB RESPONSIBILITIES</strong></h4> <ul> <li><strong>Embed and Build:</strong> Deploy directly into our clients' technical ecosystems and work with key stakeholders to understand their unique workflows, technical environments, and mission challenges. Design, build, and ship production-grade code. You will write robust Python applications, advanced SQL, and leverage our APIs to solve bespoke challenges at the edge.</li> <li><strong>Own the Outcome:</strong> Lead the entire technical lifecycle of our most strategic deployments, from initial discovery and architectural design to implementation and operationalization. You are responsible for ensuring our platform delivers tangible, mission-critical value.</li> <li><strong>Be the Tip of the Spear:</strong> Serve as the primary technical authority for our clients. You'll lead deep-dive technical workshops, debug complex systems, and act as a trusted, hands-on advisor on how to best leverage Sayari's platform to achieve their objectives in real-world investigations and missions.</li> <li><strong>Engineer Solutions for the Mission:</strong> Deconstruct ambiguous, high-level mission requirements into concrete technical solutions. Architect and implement scalable data models and workflows, ranging from building robust data pipelines into client persistence layers (Snowflake, Databricks, etc.) to developing custom Python scripts and prototyping AI/ML models.</li> <li><strong>Shape Our Product:</strong> Act as the vital conduit between the front lines and our core engineering teams. You will translate complex, real-world operational needs and feedback into the next generation of Sayari's platform.</li> </ul> <h4>SKILLS &amp; EXPERIENCE</h4> <p><em>Required:</em></p> <ul> <li>5+ years of experience in a client-facing, technical role (Forward Deployed Engineer, Sales Engineering, Solutions Architecture, Technical Consulting, etc.).</li> <li>Demonstrated ability to write clean, production-ready code, with strong proficiency in programming languages (preferably Python) and its data manipulation ecosystem (Pandas, etc.).</li> <li>Deep fluency in writing and optimizing complex SQL queries.</li> <li>Hands-on, practical experience consuming, integrating, and reasoning about RESTful APIs.</li> <li>Strong communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to bridge the gap between code and mission. You can articulate highly technical concepts with clarity and conviction to engineers, analysts, and senior leaders alike.</li> <li>Excellent problem-solving skills and the ability to navigate ambiguous, complex scenarios.</li> <li>A relentless drive and intellectual curiosity. You are comfortable parachuting into new environments and learning new technologies on the fly.</li> <li>Bachelor's or Master's degree in a technical field strongly preferred</li> </ul> <p><em>Desired:</em></p> <ul> <li>Practical experience leveraging AI, including prompting Large Language Models (LLMs) or using their APIs to build analytical solutions.</li> <li>Experience building and deploying solutions on cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, Azure) and familiarity with containerization (Docker, Kubernetes).</li> <li>Familiarity with the modern data stack (e.g., Snowflake, Databricks) and workflow orchestrators (e.g., Airflow, Prefect).</li> <li>Knowledge of graph data technologies (Neo4j, TigerGraph) and their corresponding query languages.</li> <li>Exposure to machine learning applications, particularly in NLP or entity resolution.</li> <li>Experience in, or a demonstrated passion for, high-stakes domains such as AML/Financial Crime, Supply Chain Risk, or National Security.</li> <li>Ability to travel up to 30%</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><em>The target base salary for this position is $160,000-$180,000 plus commission and equity. Final offer amounts are determined by multiple factors including location, local market variances, candidate experience and expertise, internal peer equity, and may vary from the amounts listed above.</em></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <h3><strong>Benefits:&nbsp;</strong></h3> <div> <ul> <li>100% fully paid medical, vision, and dental for employees and their dependents</li> <li>Generous time off; we observe all US federal holidays, close our office for a winter break (12/24-12/31), in addition to granting 18 PTO days and 10 sick days</li> <li>Outstanding compensation package; competitive commissions for revenue roles and bonuses for non-revenue positions</li> <li>A strong commitment to diversity, equity, and inclusion</li> <li>Eligibility to participate in additional benefits such as 401k match up to 5%, 100% paid life insurance (up to $100,000 coverage),, and parental leave</li> <li>A collaborative and positive culture - your team will be as smart and driven as you</li> <li>Limitless growth and learning opportunities</li> </ul> </div> <div>&nbsp;</div> <div><em>Sayari is an equal opportunity employer and strongly encourages diverse candidates to apply. We believe diversity and inclusion mean our team members should reflect the diversity of the United States. No employee or applicant will face discrimination or harassment based on race, color, ethnicity, religion, age, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, disability status, veteran status, genetics, or political affiliation. We strongly encourage applicants of all backgrounds to apply.</em></div><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="title">Pay Range</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$160,000</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$180,000 USD</span></div></div></div>

title: Senior Data Engineer

Headquartered in Washington, D.C., with offices in London, Singapore, Tokyo, and Tel Aviv.</p> </div></div><h3><strong>POSITION DESCRIPTION</strong></h3> <p>As a Data Engineer at Sayari, you will be the engine behind the world’s most comprehensive commercial world model. You will join a high-autonomy team responsible for building and scaling the complex orchestration systems that transform billions of primary-source records into actionable intelligence. This is a role for a "builder" who respects the complexity of large-scale ETL and graph databases and is "PhD-curious" about the future of AI-native data products and modern orchestration.</p> <h4><strong>JOB RESPONSIBILITIES</strong></h4> <ul> <li>Design, build, and maintain scalable data pipelines using Python, Spark, and Airflow to support our core data acquisition and entity resolution engines.</li> </ul> <ul> <li>Collaborate cross-functionally with AI/ML and Product teams to implement new features and AI-native products.</li> <li>Proactively identify and resolve bottlenecks in our complex ETL processes, bringing a fresh perspective to refine and optimize our existing codebase.</li> <li>Contribute to a robust engineering culture through rigorous code reviews, unit testing, and clear communication of design decisions.</li> <li>Own the end-to-end delivery of roadmap tasks within two-week sprints, ensuring work meets high standards for quality, documentation, and performance.</li> <li>Participate in roadmap planning and story refinement, eventually taking ownership of major epics that drive our long-term product defensibility.</li> </ul> <h4>SKILLS &amp; EXPERIENCE</h4> <p><strong>Required</strong></p> <ul> <li>5 or more years of production data engineering experience, with clear ownership of systems you built and operated end to end</li> <li>Strong Python, with meaningful experience in a JVM language (Scala preferred) or willingness to ramp quickly</li> <li>Hands-on Snowflake experience, or equivalent depth in BigQuery or Redshift with demonstrated ability to transfer</li> <li>Experience deploying and operating AI or ML applications in production, including output validation, monitoring, and cost management at scale</li> <li>Orchestration experience with Apache Airflow or a comparable workflow tool</li> <li>Track record of operating production systems reliably, with comfort navigating failure, monitoring, and recovery</li> </ul> <p><strong>Preferred</strong></p> <ul> <li>Experience with Spark on Dataproc Serverless or other serverless Spark environments</li> <li>Familiarity with Kubernetes for deployment</li> <li>Experience with data quality tooling such as deequ, Great Expectations, or equivalent</li> <li>GCP experience (BigQuery, Dataproc, Cloud Storage)</li> <li>Experience leading or contributing to a data warehouse migration</li> <li>Background in team mergers or migrating a team onto a new operating process</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>The target base salary for this position is $140,000-$160,000 plus company bonus and equity. Final offer amounts are determined by multiple factors including location, local market variances, candidate experience and expertise, internal peer equity, and may vary from the amounts listed above.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <h3><strong>Benefits:&nbsp;</strong></h3> <div> <ul> <li>100% fully paid medical, vision, and dental for employees and their dependents</li> <li>Generous time off; we observe all US federal holidays, close our office for a winter break (12/24-12/31), in addition to granting 18 PTO days and 10 sick days</li> <li>Outstanding compensation package; competitive commissions for revenue roles and bonuses for non-revenue positions</li> <li>A strong commitment to diversity, equity, and inclusion</li> <li>Eligibility to participate in additional benefits such as 401k match up to 5%, 100% paid life insurance (up to $100,000 coverage),, and parental leave</li> <li>A collaborative and positive culture - your team will be as smart and driven as you</li> <li>Limitless growth and learning opportunities</li> </ul> </div> <div>&nbsp;</div> <div><em>Sayari is an equal opportunity employer and strongly encourages diverse candidates to apply.
