If you need assistance or accommodation due to a disability, please contact talentacquisitionoperations@securityscorecard.io.</span></em></p> <p><em><span style="font-weight: 400;">Any information you submit to SecurityScorecard as part of your application will be processed in accordance with the Company’s privacy policy and applicable law.&nbsp;</span></em></p> <p><em><span style="font-weight: 400;">SecurityScorecard does not accept unsolicited resumes from employment agencies.&nbsp; Please note that we do not provide immigration sponsorship for this position. &nbsp; <span style="color: rgb(255, 255, 255);">#LI-DNI</span></span></em></p>

title: Data Engineering Manager
full_description: <p>Doximity is transforming the healthcare industry. Join our mission to help every physician be more productive and provide better care for their patients. As medicine's largest network in the United States, there's an elevated level of responsibility in everything we do. We don't take that responsibility lightly and are committed to building diverse teams with an inclusive culture that can make a direct impact on the healthcare system.</p> <p>One of Doximity's<a href="https://work.doximity.com/"> core values</a> is stretching ourselves. Even if you don't check off all the boxes below we encourage you to apply. Doximity is full of exceptional people who bring their own unique experiences to work everyday and make us all better for it!</p> <p><em>This role can be filled in our San Francisco headquarters OR remotely in the US or Canada.</em></p> <p><strong><em>About you</em></strong></p> <ul> <li><strong><em>Foremost an engineer</em></strong><em>.</em> You exemplify high code quality and guide others, and have at least <strong>5 years</strong> of industry experience as a data engineer or similar role.</li> <ul> <li>You are experienced in creating automated testing, applying design patterns, and other engineering best practices.</li> <li>You are an expert in Python and possess fluency in SQL and have developed maintainable data pipelines with each.</li> </ul> <li><strong><em>Experienced People Manager.</em></strong> You have experience managing 3 or more people whereby you were accountable for their growth and success as individuals and team members.</li> <li><strong><em>Communication Extraordinaire.</em></strong> You excel at communicating to both technical and non-technical people, with an ability to explain complex concepts simply.</li> <li><strong><em>Player Coach</em></strong><em>.</em> You are able to contribute individually, outside of your reports or team, balancing priorities between being a player (i.e. individual contributor) and a coach (i.e. manager or mentor).</li> <li><strong><em>See a project from end-to-end</em></strong><em>.</em> You have led several projects, your own ideas and other's. This means from idea generation to planning, execution, through delivering.</li> <li><strong><em>ML/AI–aware. </em></strong>You have working knowledge of how ML/AI applications are deployed and monitored, and you’re motivated to use this awareness to advance data-driven initiatives.</li> </ul> <p><strong><em>Here's How You Will Make an Impact</em></strong></p> <ul> <li><em>Collaborate with product managers, data analysts, and machine learning engineers to develop pipelines and ETL tasks in order to facilitate the extraction of insights.</em></li> <li><em>Establish data architecture processes and practices that can be scheduled, automated, replicated and serve as standards for other teams to leverage.</em><em>Inform data team strategy by working with the product leaders and managers. </em></li> <li><em>Actively participate in execution and some planning of organizational data team strategy. </em></li> <li><em>Collaborate with Doximity’s Data Leadership team to ensure alignment and success across the entire Data Organization.</em></li> <li><em>Manage individual Data Engineers to foster learning, growth and success at Doximity.</em></li> </ul> <p><strong><em>About Us</em></strong></p> <ul> <li><em><a href="https://technology.doximity.com/technology-stack">Explore our stack</a></em></li> <li><em>We have over 500 private repositories in Github containing our pipelines, our own internal multi-functional tools, and<a href="https://github.com/doximity"> open-source projects</a></em></li> <li><em>We have worked as a distributed team for a long time;<a href="https://blog.brunomiranda.com/building-a-distributed-engineering-team-85d281b9b1c"> we're currently about 65% distributed</a></em></li> <li><em>Find out more information </em><em>on the<a href="https://engineering.doximity.com/"> Doximity engineering blog</a></em></li> <li><em>Our<a href="https://work.doximity.com/"> company core values</a></em></li> <li><em>Our<a href="https://engineering.doximity.com/articles/engineering-recruitment-process-doximity"> recruiting process</a></em></li> <li><em>Our<a href="https://engineering.doximity.com/articles/mofo-driven-product-development"> product development cycle</a></em></li> <li><em>Our<a href="https://engineering.doximity.com/articles/software-engineering-on-boarding-at-doximity"> on-boarding &amp; mentorship process</a></em></li> </ul> <p><strong><em>Compensation</em></strong></p> <p><em>The US total compensation range for this full-time position is $172,000 - $254,000 (i</em><em>nclusive of salary + equity). Our ranges are determined by role and level. The range displayed on each job posting reflects the approximate total target compensation for the position across the US. Within the range, individual pay is determined by factors including relevant skills, experience, and education/training. Please note that the compensation listed does not include benefits.</em></p> <p><strong><em>More on /Benefits/Perks&nbsp;</em></strong></p> <p><em>Doximity is proud to offer industry-leading benefits to our full time employees. Some of our offerings include:&nbsp;</em></p> <ul> <li><em>Medical, dental, vision offerings for you and your family</em></li> <li><em>4</em><em>01k with matching program</em></li> <li><em>Employee stock purchase plan</em></li> <li><em>Family planning support, Childcare FSA, and parental leave</em></li> <li><em>Life, AD&amp;D, and Disability</em></li> <li><em>Generous time off, holidays and paid company trips</em></li> <li><em>Wellness benefits…plus many more!</em></li> </ul> <p><strong><em>More About Doximity…</em></strong></p> <p><em>For the past decade, it’s been our mission to help every physician be more productive so they can provide better care for their patients. We believe that when doctors are connected, the healthcare system works better and patients benefit. Doximity enables our verified clinician members to collaborate with colleagues, stay up-to-date with the latest medical news and research, manage their careers, and conduct virtual patient visits. Today, Doximity is the leading digital platform for U.S. medical professionals, with over 85% of physicians, 50% of all nurse practitioners and physician assistants, and 90% of graduating medical students as members.&nbsp;</em></p> <p><em>Joining Doximity means being part of an incredibly talented and humble team passionate about improving inefficiencies in our $4.3 trillion U.S. healthcare system. We are a team of doers who solve problems everyday by treating obstacles like an adventure, and we love creating technology that has a real, meaningful impact on people’s lives. Doxers are committed to working towards a more equitable world both within and beyond our office walls. This starts by fostering an inclusive and diverse work environment where differences are valued and all employees are encouraged to bring their full, authentic selves to work daily. To learn more about our team, culture, and users, check out our</em><a href="https://workat.doximity.com/"><em> </em><em>careers page</em></a><em>,</em><a href="https://blog.doximity.com/"><em> </em><em>company blog</em></a><em>, and</em><a href="https://engineering.doximity.com/"><em> </em><em>engineering blog</em></a><em>. We’re growing fast, and there’s plenty of opportunity for you to make an impact—join us! For more information, visit</em><a href="http://www.doximity.com/"><em> Doximity.com</em></a><em>.</em></p> <p><em>____________________________________________</em></p> <p><strong><em>EEOC Statement</em></strong></p> <p><em>Doximity is proud to be an equal opportunity employer, and committed to providing employment opportunities regardless of race, religious creed, color, national origin, ancestry, physical disability, mental disability, medical condition, genetic information, marital status, sex, gender, gender identity, gender expression, pregnancy, childbirth and breastfeeding, age, sexual orientation, military or veteran status, or any other protected classification. We also consider qualified applicants with criminal histories, consistent with applicable federal, state and local law.</em></p> <p>&nbsp;</p>

title: Data Engineering Manager
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p>SmarterDx, a <a href="https://www.smartertech.com/home-p" target="_blank">Smarter Technologies</a> company, builds clinical AI that is transforming how hospitals translate care into payment. Founded by physicians in 2020, our platform connects clinical context with revenue intelligence, helping health systems recover millions in missed revenue, improve quality scores, and appeal every denial. Become a Smartian and help optimize the way the healthcare system works for everyone. Learn more at&nbsp;<a class="c-link" href="http://smarterdx.com/careers" target="_blank" data-stringify-link="http://smarterdx.com/careers" data-sk="tooltip_parent">smarterdx.com/careers</a>.</p></div><h3>Role</h3> <p>SmarterDx is seeking a Manager of Data Engineering, Integrations to lead and grow a high‑performing data engineering team responsible for ingesting, harmonizing, and operationalizing healthcare data (EHR, clinical, and claims) across our products. You will scale new‑client onboarding, raise the bar on data quality and governance, and ensure resilient, observable, and cost‑effective ELT pipelines. You will set clear objectives, mentor engineers, and partner closely with Product, Clinical, Data Science, and Engineering to deliver durable outcomes.</p> <p>SmarterDx builds clinical AI that empowers hospitals to analyze the complete record of every patient to fully capture the value of care delivered. Founded by physicians in 2020, its proprietary AI platform understands the nuances of clinical reasoning, enabling hospitals to true the patient record for every discharge. By doing so, hospitals can recover millions in earned revenue, enhance care quality metrics, and optimize healthcare operations. Become a Smartian and help optimize the way the healthcare system works for everyone. Learn more at smarterdx.com/careers.</p> <p><em>**This role is fully remote within the US**</em></p> <p><strong>What You'll Do</strong></p> <ul> <li>Lead, coach, and develop a team of analytics engineers and/or data engineers with a focus on technical excellence, delivery velocity, and collaborative culture.</li> <li>Ensure on-time delivery of client data integrations by owning enterprise data model standards and maintaining consistent, governed data definitions.</li> <li>Oversee client data pipelines using modern tooling (dbt, Airflow, Snowflake, AWS, Python) to ensure reliable operation and uptime.</li> <li>Drive client integration delivery speed by implementing automation, reusable patterns, and clear runbooks to directly optimize client onboarding timelines.</li> <li>Establish and enforce SLOs/SLAs, instrument critical paths, and drive incident reduction via proactive monitoring, alerting, and post‑incident reviews.</li> <li>Implement comprehensive data quality and testing frameworks (schema, nullability, referential integrity, reconciliation) and lineage observability.</li> <li>Collaborate cross‑functionally (Product, Clinical, Data Science, Engineering) to prioritize roadmaps, land measurable outcomes, and manage tradeoffs.</li> <li>Manage high-volume client integration requests, triaging and prioritizing tickets based on client impact, urgency, and business value while maintaining clear communication on resolution timelines.</li> <li>Own daily operations rhythm including ticket review, resource allocation, escalation management, and stakeholder updates to ensure client integration commitments are met.</li> <li>Drive operational metrics and reporting on integration delivery performance, ticket resolution times, and team capacity to identify bottlenecks and improve client outcomes</li> </ul> <p><strong>What You Bring</strong></p> <ul> <li>7+ years of data engineering experience, including leadership of engineers and outcomes (hiring, mentoring, performance, delivery).</li> <li>Deep experience with healthcare datasets: clinical, and/or billing/claims; familiarity with HL7/FHIR and interoperability patterns.</li> <li>Strong SQL and data modeling expertise; proven track record building reliable ELT in dbt, Airflow, Snowflake, and Python.</li> <li>An Operational excellence mindset that integrates SLOs/SLAs, on‑call/incident management, root‑cause analysis, and preventive improvements.</li> <li>Demonstrated ability to accelerate client data onboarding through automation, standardization, and repeatable integration patterns.</li> <li>Excellent communication and stakeholder management; comfortable driving clarity and alignment across Product, Clinical, and technical partners.</li> <li>Willingness to roll up sleeves and contribute hands‑on to unblock the team.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Nice To Haves</strong></p> <ul> <li>Experience at a scale‑up or rapid‑growth technology company.</li> <li>Experience leading product implementations and complex client data integrations.</li> <li>Hands‑on interoperability experience (HL7, FHIR, CCD/C-CDA), EHR extract patterns, and healthcare data normalization.</li> <li>Certifications: Snowflake (SnowPro), AWS (Data Analytics or Solutions Architect), dbt, or healthcare data (CHDA/RHIA).</li> </ul> <p><strong>Our Tech Stack</strong></p> <ul> <li>Databases: Snowflake (primary), Postgres</li> <li>Cloud Infrastructure: AWS (CDK, S3, MWAA, EventBridge, Batch, Lambda, Step Functions), FiveTran</li> <li>Technologies: SQL, dbt, Python</li> </ul> <h3><strong>Compensation</strong></h3> <p>$190,000 - $210,000</p> <p>#LI-Remote</p><div class="content-conclusion"><h3>Benefits</h3> <ul class="p-rich_text_list p-rich_text_list__bullet p-rich_text_list--nested" data-stringify-type="unordered-list" data-list-tree="true" data-indent="0" data-border="0"> <li data-stringify-indent="0" data-stringify-border="0"><strong data-stringify-type="bold">Medical, Dental &amp; Vision</strong> – Comprehensive plans with leading insurance providers, covering 75% of your premiums, depending on the plan.</li> <li data-stringify-indent="0" data-stringify-border="0"><strong data-stringify-type="bold">Paid Parental Leave</strong> – Generous paid leave to support families through birth or adoption: Up to 12 weeks for parents.</li> <li data-stringify-indent="0" data-stringify-border="0"><strong data-stringify-type="bold">Remote-First Team</strong> – Work from anywhere in the U.S.</li> <li data-stringify-indent="0" data-stringify-border="0"><strong data-stringify-type="bold">Unlimited PTO &amp; 10 Holidays</strong> – So you can relax and recharge.</li> <li data-stringify-indent="0" data-stringify-border="0"><strong data-stringify-type="bold">401(k) with Traditional &amp; Roth Options&nbsp;</strong>– Tax-advantaged retirement savings through Fidelity with a 4% match.</li> <li data-stringify-indent="0" data-stringify-border="0"><strong data-stringify-type="bold">Minimal Bureaucracy</strong>&nbsp;– A fast-moving, high-impact environment where you can focus on what matters.</li> <li data-stringify-indent="0" data-stringify-border="0"><strong data-stringify-type="bold">Incredible Teammates!</strong>&nbsp;– Work alongside smart, supportive, and mission-driven colleagues.</li> </ul></div>

title: Cyber Data Engineer
full_description: <p><strong>**ACTIVE TS/SCI SECURITY CLEARANCE REQUIRED**</strong></p> <p>Build the infrastructure that keeps critical national security data flowing. You'll own the pipelines, systems, and integrations that power real cybersecurity investigations — and have the autonomy to solve complex engineering problems that actually matter.</p> <table style="border-collapse: collapse; width: 624px; border-style: solid;" border="1" width="624"> <tbody> <tr> <td width="104"> <p><strong>Linux / Windows</strong></p> </td> <td width="104"> <p><strong>SIEM (Splunk / Elastic)</strong></p> </td> <td width="104"> <p><strong>VMware / Hyper-V</strong></p> </td> <td width="104"> <p><strong>Networking &amp; Firewalls</strong></p> </td> <td width="104"> <p><strong>Python / Bash</strong></p> </td> <td width="104"> <p><strong>Cloud (AWS)</strong></p> </td> </tr> </tbody> </table> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>WHAT YOU'LL OWN</strong></p> <table style="border-collapse: collapse; width: 624px; border-width: 1px; border-style: inset;" border="1" width="624"> <tbody> <tr> <td style="border-width: 1px;" width="312"> <p><strong>Data Pipelines</strong></p> <p>Ingest, normalize, and manage event logs powering live security investigations.</p> </td> <td style="border-width: 1px;" width="312"> <p><strong>SIEM Management</strong></p> <p>Own Splunk, Elastic, or ArcSight deployments end-to-end — configs, patches, uptime.</p> </td> </tr> <tr> <td style="border-width: 1px;" width="312"> <p><strong>Host &amp; VM Administration</strong></p> <p>Linux and Windows systems, hypervisors (ESXi, Hyper-V), and cloud assets.</p> </td> <td style="border-width: 1px;" width="312"> <p><strong>Security Compliance</strong></p> <p>Apply federal, DoD, and IC configuration standards across all managed systems.</p> </td> </tr> </tbody> </table> <p><strong>WHAT YOU'LL NEED</strong></p> <ul> <li>5+ years in systems engineering or a similar infrastructure/ops role</li> <li>Advanced Linux proficiency — comfortable on the command line, can work through complex issues independently</li> <li>Strong networking fundamentals: DNS, DHCP, IPAM, load balancers, firewalls</li> <li>Hands-on hypervisor experience (VMware ESXi, Hyper-V, or Citrix XenServer)</li> <li>IAT II certification (or ability to obtain); CSSP Infrastructure Support cert required within 120 days of hire</li> <li>Proven root-cause analysis skills — you don't just fix symptoms, you find the source</li> <li>Excellent communication skills; comfortable working with both technical peers and non-technical stakeholders</li> </ul> <p><strong>BONUS POINTS</strong></p> <ul> <li>SIEM experience with Splunk, Elastic, or ArcSight</li> <li>Familiarity with Cribl for data aggregation and normalization</li> <li>Scripting in Python, Bash, or PowerShell</li> <li>Public cloud experience (AWS, GCP, Rackspace) or private cloud (VMware, OpenStack)</li> </ul> <p><strong>Additional Information</strong></p> <ul> <li>All your information will be kept confidential according to EEO guidelines.</li> <li>Compensation is unique to each candidate and relative to the skills and experience they bring to the position. The salary range for this position is typically $140-$145k. This does not guarantee a specific salary as compensation is based upon multiple factors such as education, experience, certifications, and other requirements, and may fall outside of the above-stated range.</li> <li>Highlights of our benefits include Health/Dental/Vision, 401(k) match, Accrued PTO, STD/LTD/Life Insurance, Referral Bonuses, professional development reimbursement, and more!</li> </ul> <p><em>D2 Technical Services is committed to a merit-based recruitment process and encourages applications from all qualified individuals.&nbsp; As a Veteran-Owned Small Business, we particularly welcome applications from veterans who have the requisite skills and experience.&nbsp; Job applicants that are interested in one of our openings and may require a reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, should contact us to request an accommodation.</em></p> <p>&nbsp;</p>

title: Data Engineer
full_description: <p><strong><span data-olk-copy-source="MessageBody">What Impact You’ll Have</span></strong></p> <p>GRVTY’s team provides tactical data engineering solutions. We embed skilled Data&nbsp;Engineers, Data Scientists, and ETL Developers directly into intelligence analyst groups&nbsp;to be their go-to data wranglers. We develop new tools, code, and services to execute&nbsp;data engineering activities. Our engineers work to collect, process, and feed analytic&nbsp;tools, turning data into intelligence in response to immediate mission needs, with direct&nbsp;impact on real world situations. You will see your work used here on a daily basis, and&nbsp;you'll have the opportunity to support a variety of Sponsor mission organizations and&nbsp;mission partner organizations.<br>This is a time of development and growth on the program, with an increasing number of missions being supported. The work is high impact and important, and the customer moves quickly. The environment is fast-paced, flexible, and open to innovation - you'll have more latitude here in choosing how to achieve results than on many other projects. The customer cares more about what you can do as opposed to your years of<br>experience, and work hours are typically quite flexible - roll up your sleeves, get things&nbsp;done, and no one cares much about the specific hours that you work. The work space&nbsp;itself is also quite nice, and there is an excellent cafeteria!&nbsp;The tech stack on this team is rather huge and includes Python (Pandas, numpy, scipy,&nbsp;scikit-learn, standard libraries, etc.), Python packages that wrap Machine Learning&nbsp;(packages for NLP, Object Detection, etc.), Linux, AWS/C2S, Apache NiFi, Spark,&nbsp;pySpark, Hadoop, Kafka, ElasticSearch, Solr, Kibana, neo4J, MariaDB, Postgres,&nbsp;Docker, Puppet, and many others.&nbsp;Work on this program takes place in McLean, VA and in various field offices throughout&nbsp;Northern VA (we cannot support remote work) and requires a TS/SCI + Polygraph&nbsp;clearance (acceptable to this customer).</p> <p><strong><span data-olk-copy-source="MessageBody">What You’ll Be Owning</span></strong></p> <p>GRVTY is seeking a Data Engineer with a TS/SCI + Poly clearance (applicable to this customer) to join one of our top projects in Chantilly, VA.&nbsp;&nbsp;</p> <p>Responsibilities</p> <ul> <li>Develop new tools, code &amp; services to execute data engineering activities. Activities include the following tasks:&nbsp;</li> <li>Movement of structure &amp; unstructured data using approved methods. Execute data ingestion activities for storing data in a local or enterprise level location. View data in its source format.</li> <li>Develop code to format data that supports exploration. Analyze source data formats &amp; work with Data Scientists &amp; partners to determine the formats &amp; transforms that best meet mission objectives.</li> <li>Develop code and tools to provide one-time &amp; on-going data formatting &amp; transformations into enterprise or boutique data models. Implement existing ETL code &amp; best practices/standards.</li> <li>Develop an ETL Code Transition Plan. Develop &amp; deliver documentation for each project including ETL mappings, code use guide, code location &amp; access instructions.</li> <li>Facilitate Code Reviews. Provide consulting services to support data transport, ingestion, conditioning, access, &amp; management.&nbsp;</li> </ul> <p><strong>What You Must Have</strong></p> <ul> <li>Active TS/SCI with Polygraph Clearance</li> <li>Ability and willingness to quickly learn a new tool&nbsp;</li> <li>Strong communication skills with both your teammates, and your leads&nbsp;</li> <li>SQL, Python, Pyspark experience.&nbsp;</li> <li>Willingness to do development type work when needed (junior level development at best)</li> <li>Ability to be a self starter and ask questions when needed&nbsp;</li> <li>Comfortable working with and manipulating data in compliance with the offices workflow</li> <li data-ogsc="black">Extract, Transform and Load (ETL) tools and processes.&nbsp;&nbsp;</li> <li data-ogsc="black">AWS&nbsp;</li> <li data-ogsc="black">APIs&nbsp;</li> <li data-ogsc="black">Linux&nbsp;</li> <li data-ogsc="black">Geospatial tools/data<em data-ogsc="">&nbsp;</em></li> </ul> <p class="x_MsoNormal"><strong>What Would Be Nice to Have</strong></p> <ul type="disc"> <li data-ogsc="black" data-olk-copy-source="MessageBody">Palantir Foundry Experience</li> <li data-ogsc="black">Kubeflow&nbsp;</li> <li data-ogsc="black">Experience with OCR and text extraction of PDFs.&nbsp;</li> <li data-ogsc="black">Experience with data validation / data quality after ETLing to be sure it’s ready for end users</li> <li data-ogsc="black">Docker</li> <li data-ogsc="black">Jenkins</li> <li data-ogsc="black">Hadoop/Spark</li> <li data-ogsc="black">Kibana</li> <li data-ogsc="black">Kafka</li> <li data-ogsc="black">NiFi</li> <li data-ogsc="black">ElasticSearch&nbsp;&nbsp;</li> </ul><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p></p> <p><strong>Pay Range: </strong>At GRVTY, we understand that compensation is influenced by many factors—such as geographic location, federal contract labor categories, wage rates, prior experience, skillsets, education, and certifications.<br>We’re proud to offer a work environment that empowers our team to achieve a strong work-life balance. GRVTY provides competitive pay, comprehensive benefits, and meaningful opportunities for professional growth.<br>Our benefits package is designed to support the well-being of our employees and their families, and includes coverage in areas such as healthcare, financial wellness, retirement planning, family assistance, continued education, and paid time off.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p></p></div><div class="title">Virginia Pay Range</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$150,000</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$200,000 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><p id="isPasted"><strong>Why Choose GRVTY</strong></p> <hr> <p>The toughest national security challenges demand vision and ingenuity, not just resources. We deliver mission and technical expertise to outpace our adversaries. We’re purpose-built to tackle the most entrenched, systemic national security issues around the world.</p> <p>We partner with our customers to help them overcome challenges in every corner of technology and defense—including the ones still being explored. Our growing capabilities create complementary advantages, giving on-the-ground operations the edge they need to succeed. We muster everything we have to answer every challenge presented, every day of our lives.</p> <p>At GRVTY, we believe that when our employees thrive, our company thrives. That’s why we offer a comprehensive and competitive benefits package designed to support your well-being, growth, and work-life balance.</p> <p>• &nbsp; &nbsp;Robust health plan including medical, dental, and vision &nbsp;</p> <p>• &nbsp; &nbsp;Health Savings Account with company contribution &nbsp;</p> <p>• &nbsp; &nbsp;Annual Paid Time Off and Paid Holidays &nbsp;</p> <p>• &nbsp; &nbsp;Paid Parental Leave &nbsp;&nbsp;</p> <p>• &nbsp; &nbsp;401k with generous company match &nbsp;</p> <p>• &nbsp; &nbsp;Training and Development Opportunities &nbsp;</p> <p>• &nbsp; &nbsp;Award Programs &nbsp;</p> <p>• &nbsp; &nbsp;Variety of Company Sponsored Events</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>EEO Statement</strong></p> <hr> <p>GRVTY, is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, or status as a protected veteran and will not be discriminated against on the basis of disability.&nbsp;</p> <p>Anyone requiring reasonable accommodations should email <a href="mailto:recruiting@roycegeo.com" target="_blank">recruiting@grvty.com</a> or call 703-544-7930 with requested details. A member of the HR team will respond to your request within 2 business days. </p> <p><a href="https://www.eeoc.gov/sites/default/files/2022-10/EEOC_KnowYourRights_screen_reader_10_20.pdf" target="_blank">Know Your Rights: Workplace Discrimination is Illegal (eeoc.gov)</a> &nbsp;</p> <p>Please review our current job openings and apply for the positions you believe may be a fit. If you are not an immediate fit, we will also keep your resume in our database for future opportunities.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>&nbsp;</p></div>

title: Enterprise Data Engineer
full_description: <p><strong>What Impact You'll Have</strong></p> <p><span class="macro">GRVTY provides tactical data engineering solutions. We embed skilled Data Engineers, Data Scientists, and ETL Developers directly into intelligence analyst groups to be their go-to data wranglers. We develop new tools, code, and services to execute data engineering activities. Our engineers work to collect, process, and feed analytic tools, turning data into intelligence in response to immediate mission needs, with direct impact on real world situations. You will see your work used here on a daily basis, and you'll have the opportunity to support a variety of Sponsor mission organizations and mission partner organizations.</span></p> <p><strong>What You'll be Owning</strong></p> <p>GRVTY is seeking an Enterprise Data Engineer with a TS/SCI + Poly clearance (applicable to this customer) to join one of our top projects in Chantilly, VA.&nbsp; The successful Enterprise Data Engineer must be a skilled and detail-oriented professional with expertise in utilizing Python, SQL, and&nbsp;Palantir (or other ETL platforms) to extract, transform, and load structured and unstructured data from various sources.&nbsp; They must have a strong background in data engineering and a deep understanding of ETL processes.</p> <p>In this role you will:</p> <ul> <li>Write robust and efficient Python&nbsp;and&nbsp;SQL scripts to automate data processing tasks</li> <li>Integrate data from diverse sources, such as databases, APIs, and flat files, into centralized systems</li> <li>Implement data transformations, enrichment, and modeling workflows to ensure data quality and usability</li> <li>Ensure data structures are optimized for performance and scalability</li> <li>Provide technical guidance and support to ensure effective use of Palantir tools across teams</li> <li>Implement data quality checks, validation, and monitoring mechanisms to ensure the accuracy and reliability of datasets</li> <li>Integrate data from diverse sources, such as databases, APIs, and flat files, into centralized systems</li> <li>Ensure proper data transformation and curation to meet business and analytics requirements</li> <li>Maintain and improve data quality, consistency, and accuracy through validation and cleansing processes</li> </ul> <p><strong>What You Must Have</strong></p> <ul> <li>Active TS/SCI with Polygraph Clearance (applicable to this customer)</li> <li>3+ years of experience in data engineering</li> <li>Strong proficiency in scripting and programming with&nbsp;Python</li> <li>Expertise in working with&nbsp;SQL&nbsp;for data querying, transformation, and optimization</li> <li>Experience with&nbsp;data integration from diverse sources such as APIs, relational databases, and file systems</li> <li>Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with attention to detail</li> <li>Excellent communication and teamwork skills to collaborate with technical and business teams</li> </ul> <p><strong>What Would be Nice to Have</strong></p> <ul> <li>Bachelor’s degree&nbsp;in Computer Science, Data Engineering, or a related field</li> <li>Hands-on experience with&nbsp;Palantir</li> <li>Experience with&nbsp;cloud platforms(e.g., AWS, Azure, GCP) and big data technologies (e.g., Apache Spark, Hadoop)</li> <li>Familiarity with&nbsp;data orchestration toolssuch as Apache Airflow, Prefect, or similar</li> </ul> <p>#LI-BPJ</p> <p>&nbsp;</p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div rates, prior experience, skillsets, education, and certifications.<br>We’re proud to offer a work environment that empowers our team to achieve a strong work-life balance. QRT’s culture of innovation continuously drives our ambition to deliver high quality returns for our investors. </span><span data-ccp-props="{}">&nbsp;</span></p> <p><span data-contrast="none">Over the years, QRT has invested in a global research and execution platform which has been deployed to cover all geographies and asset classes. You will collaborate closely with Traders, Researchers, and Engineers to ensure our data infrastructure is robust, scalable, and able to power cutting-edge quantitative research. Your work will involve tackling complex challenges in data acquisition, engineering, and platform design.</span><span data-ccp-props="{}">&nbsp;</span></p> <p><span data-contrast="none">Examples of potential roles and projects include:</span><span data-ccp-props="{}">&nbsp;</span></p> <ul> <li data-leveltext="" data-font="Symbol" data-listid="1" data-list-defn-props="{&quot;335551671&quot;:0,&quot;335552541&quot;:1,&quot;335559685&quot;:720,&quot;335559991&quot;:360,&quot;469769226&quot;:&quot;Symbol&quot;,&quot;469769242&quot;:[8226],&quot;469777803&quot;:&quot;left&quot;,&quot;469777804&quot;:&quot;&quot;,&quot;469777815&quot;:&quot;multilevel&quot;}" data-aria-posinset="0" data-aria-level="1"><strong><span data-contrast="none">Quantitative Data Analyst</span></strong><span data-ccp-props="{}">&nbsp;</span></li> </ul> <p><span data-contrast="none">Source, design, and onboard new datasets in line with priorities set by trading desks. title: Treasury Data Engineer
full_description: <p>Qube Research &amp; Technologies (QRT) is a global quantitative and systematic investment manager, operating in all liquid asset classes across the world. We are a technology and data driven group implementing a scientific approach to investing. Combining data, research, technology, and trading expertise has shaped QRT's collaborative mindset which enables us to solve the most complex challenges. QRT's culture of innovation continuously drives our ambition to deliver high quality returns for our investors.</p> <p>The Treasury function is the financing backbone of QRT, managing the firm’s funding, collateral, margin, cash balances, and counterparty relationships across geographies and asset classes. The Treasury Analytics team builds the data infrastructure and tooling that gives the desk visibility into financing costs, margin requirements, and cash movements. The systems we build direct inform how the firm deploys its capital.</p> <p>Join our Treasury Analytics team in London as a Data Engineer, supporting and evolving the data infrastructure that powers Treasury's analytics platform, enabling the desk to analyse and optimise how the firm finances its positions in real time.</p> <p><strong>Role responsibilities</strong></p> <ul> <li>Design, build and maintain data pipelines to ingest, transform, and serve Treasury data across multiple sources and analytics systems</li> <li>Develop and maintain analytical tools to optimise margin, collateral, cash balances, and financing positions across counterparties</li> <li>Build the datasets and APIs that power Treasury's analytics platform, enabling the desk to optimise financing costs, monitor counterparty exposure, and manage liquidity in real time</li> <li>Evolve the infrastructure as the desk grows by onboarding new data sources, adapting to new requirements, and improving performance and reliability over time</li> <li>Onboard new storage and modelling layers, leveraging the right frameworks for different workloads</li> <li>Collaborate with desk leadership, traders, and engineers to implement robust, maintainable solutions</li> </ul> <p><strong>Required experience and skills</strong></p> <ul> <li>3–6 years' experience building high-performance intraday data applications, ideally in financial services</li> <li>Strong SQL, Python, and data modelling skills</li> <li>Understanding of data storage formats and trade-offs</li> <li>Experience with query optimisation and performance tuning</li> <li>Experience with modern orchestration frameworks (e.g. Airflow), cloud platforms, backend web services and networking protocols</li> <li>Knowledge of streaming systems and real-time data applications a plus</li> <li>Strong technical communication skills and ability to work effectively with a variety of stakeholders</li> </ul> <p><strong>Location</strong></p> <p>London</p>

title: Forward Deployed Product Manager – AI
location: Cambridge, Massachusetts, United States, New York, New York, United States
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Iterative Health is a healthcare technology and services company powering the acceleration of clinical research to transform patient outcomes.</span></p> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;">We built a leading performance-driven network of 100+ sites across the US, Europe, India, and Australia, conducting research directly in the communities where care is delivered across gastrointestinal, hepatology, obesity, and cardiology. By combining deep clinical trial expertise with cutting-edge AI, we connect sponsors' scientific ambitions with high-performing research teams that expedite and expand access to novel therapeutics for patients in need. Today, Iterative Health is headquartered in Cambridge, Massachusetts, and New York City with 250+ employees world-wide.</span></p> <p>&nbsp;</p></div><p><span style="font-size: 10pt; font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">At<a href="https://iterative.health/?utm_source=chatgpt.com"> Iterative Health</a>, we are transforming clinical research through the power of AI, technology, and human expertise. Iterative Health is a healthcare technology and services company powering the acceleration of clinical research to improve patient outcomes. Through the Iterative Health Site Network — a premier network of 100+ clinical research sites across the US and Europe — we help expand and expedite access to novel therapeutics for patients in need.</span></p> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt; font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Accelerating clinical research is one of the defining challenges in healthcare. Promising therapies exist that patients can't access because the operational infrastructure to run clinical trials efficiently doesn't exist yet. We're building it.</span></p> <h2><span style="font-size: 10pt; font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><strong>Why This Role Matters</strong></span></h2> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">As a Forward Deployed AI Product Manager at Iterative Health, you will work cross functionally to identify, build, and deploy AI-powered solutions that transform how clinical research operations are executed. </span><span style="font-size: 10pt;">This is a highly hands-on role that sits at the intersection of product, engineering, and operations. You will partner closely with end users to understand their workflows, identify opportunities where AI can create meaningful leverage, rapidly prototype solutions, and deploy them into production. </span><span style="font-size: 10pt;">The ideal candidate combines strong product instincts with technical fluency and a builder mentality. You are equally comfortable sitting alongside a clinical operator to understand a workflow as you are working with engineers and AI tooling to bring a solution to life.</span></span></p> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">You will work across Clinical Operations, Site Operations, Trial Enrollment, Business Development, and Corporate Functions, identifying high-impact opportunities and owning them from concept through deployment and adoption. </span><span style="font-size: 10pt;">This role reports to the Product organization and partners closely with Engineering, Data Science, Security, and Operational Leadership teams.</span></span></p> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt; font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-size: 10pt; font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Demonstrated ownership mindset and bias toward action</span></li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt; font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><strong>Preferred</strong></span></p> <ul> <li style="font-size: 10pt; font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-size: 10pt; font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Experience in healthcare, clinical research, or another regulated domain (HIPAA, GxP, financial services)</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt; font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-size: 10pt; font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Background as a technical founder, early engineer at a startup, or in a customer-facing engineering role (FDE, solutions engineering, applied AI)</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt; font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-size: 10pt; font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Interest in growing toward a senior product role over time — this role is designed as a natural bridge</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt; font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-size: 10pt; font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Experience with agent frameworks (Claude Agent SDK, LangGraph, AgentCore, etc.), MCP servers, or production LLM evaluation tooling</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt; font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-size: 10pt; font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Familiarity with EMR systems, clinical trial operations, or healthcare data standards (FHIR, OMOP)</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt; font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-size: 10pt; font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Experience working with messy, real-world data and turning it into something useful without pretending it's clean</span></li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <h2><span style="font-size: 10pt; font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><strong>Benefits That Support You</strong></span></h2> <h2><span style="font-size: 10pt; font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">We believe great teams do their best work when they feel supported — professionally and personally.</span></h2> <ul> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-size: 10pt; font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Hybrid work environment with in-office collaboration two days per week in either our NYC or Boston office</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-size: 10pt; font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision coverage, with up to 80% of premiums covered by Iterative Health</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-size: 10pt; font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Mental health and wellness support through Spring Health</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-size: 10pt; font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Health HSA or FSA options, and commuter FSA contributions supported by Iterative Health</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-size: 10pt; font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Unlimited PTO, 12 company holidays, and a company-wide shutdown between Christmas and New Years</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-size: 10pt; font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">401(k) program with a company match of up to 3% (up to $3,000 annually)</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-size: 10pt; font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Weekly in-office lunch benefit every Tuesday</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-size: 10pt; font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">100% company-paid short-term and long-term disability coverage</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-size: 10pt; font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Annual wellness and professional development stipend to support your health and growth</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-size: 10pt; font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">And more!</span></li> </ul> <h2><span style="font-size: 10pt; font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">At Iterative Health, we believe great people drive innovation. If you’re excited about helping transform the future of clinical research while building scalable people strategies that support growth and impact, we encourage you to apply.</span></h2> <p><br><br></p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="title">New York pay range</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$170,000</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$250,000 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><p><span style="font-size: 10pt;">At Iterative Health, we’re actively working towards creating an environment that is representative of the diversity of patients our technology serves. We are focused on building an equitable and inclusive culture, and by extension, hiring process. If you require any accommodations to make the application process or interviewing experience more accessible to you, please contact CandidateAccommodations@iterative.health.</span></p></div>

title: Staff Data Engineer
location: Cambridge, Massachusetts, United States, New York, New York, United States
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Iterative Health is a healthcare technology and services company powering the acceleration of clinical research to transform patient outcomes.</span></p> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;">We built a leading performance-driven network of 100+ sites across the US, Europe, India, and Australia, conducting research directly in the communities where care is delivered across gastrointestinal, hepatology, obesity, and cardiology. By combining deep clinical trial expertise with cutting-edge AI, we connect sponsors' scientific ambitions with high-performing research teams that expedite and expand access to novel therapeutics for patients in need. Today, Iterative Health is headquartered in Cambridge, Massachusetts, and New York City with 250+ employees world-wide.</span></p> <p>&nbsp;</p></div><p><strong>About the Role</strong></p> <p>Accelerating clinical research is one of the defining challenges in healthcare. Promising therapies exist that patients can't access because the operational infrastructure to run clinical trials efficiently doesn't exist yet. We're building it. That means designing technology systems that bring order to a fragmented landscape of clinical data sources, automating the operational work that slows trials down, and turning real-world clinical data into a foundation for predictive intelligence.</p> <p>We're building a uniquely valuable data asset: real-world patient and research data flowing across 80+ trial sites, spanning dozens of EHRs and clinical systems, focused on patient populations that are chronically underserved by existing clinical research infrastructure. Your job is to build the pipelines, data models, and AI infrastructure that make this asset real, from ingestion and normalization through to the systems that power predictions on top of it. You'll own data quality and observability as foundational engineering problems. You'll also have a direct hand in shaping how this data drives our AI strategy, what we model, what we predict, and what becomes possible.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>This is an opportunity for someone who wants to be part of a small, fast-moving engineering team at a formative stage. You'll shape what gets built, how decisions get made, and what the team becomes.</p> <p><strong>Responsibilities</strong></p> <ul> <li>Own the data layer and architecture:&nbsp; the models, schemas, and infrastructure decisions that everything downstream depends on</li> <li>Build and operate the pipelines and transformations that move data from ingestion through normalization, enrichment, and into the formats that support analytics, ML training, and production model serving</li> <li>Own data quality and observability: build the systems that make data issues visible and correctable before they compound</li> <li>Partner with ML and engineering teams to identify what's modelable, define training data requirements, and build the data foundations for new predictive capabilities</li> <li>Define how clinical and operational data is governed across the system</li> <li>Evaluate and select the tools and technologies that make up the data stack, with a clear point of view on build vs. buy</li> <li>Help shape the engineering culture of a small, growing team: how technical decisions get made, how problems get debated, what rigor looks like in practice</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>What We’re Looking For</strong></p> <p>Required Qualifications</p> <ul> <li>10+ years of experience in data engineering or related roles, with significant time spent building data systems</li> <li>Experience with healthcare data strongly preferred (HL7, FHIR, claims, EHR extracts) or other complex, regulated data domains</li> <li>Deep experience modeling and integrating data from multiple heterogeneous sources with inconsistent schemas and quality</li> <li>Experience applying AI and LLMs to data engineering problems: extraction, normalization, classification, entity resolution</li> <li>Strong understanding of how data infrastructure supports ML workflows from feature engineering to training data pipelines to model serving</li> <li>Fluent in SQL and at least one modern programming language (Python, Java, Scala, Go), with experience across modern data infrastructure - distributed processing, streaming, cloud-native storage, orchestration, and transformation frameworks</li> <li>Have built data systems from early stages, making foundational decisions with incomplete information</li> <li>Naturally raise the quality of the engineering around you through code review, design guidance, and honest technical conversation</li> </ul> <p>Preferred Qualifications</p> <ul> <li>Experience building data infrastructure that directly supports ML model training and evaluation</li> <li>Familiarity with clinical trial operations, EDC systems, or life sciences data</li> <li>SOC 2, HIPAA or similar compliance experience baked into engineering practice</li> <li>A track record of building or improving data systems that others had given up on making reliable</li> </ul><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="title">New York pay range</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$200,000</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$325,000 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><p><span style="font-size: 10pt;">At Iterative Health, we’re actively working towards creating an environment that is representative of the diversity of patients our technology serves. We are focused on building an equitable and inclusive culture, and by extension, hiring process. If you require any accommodations to make the application process or interviewing experience more accessible to you, please contact CandidateAccommodations@iterative.health.</span></p></div>

title: Senior Data Engineer
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><h2><span data-ccp-props="{&quot;134233117&quot;:true,&quot;134233118&quot;:true,&quot;201341983&quot;:0,&quot;335559740&quot;:240}">About Hopscotch Primary Care</span></h2> <p>At Hopscotch Primary Care, we believe great healthcare should be accessible to all people across all communities.&nbsp; Today, almost 20% of Americans live in a rural community, yet only 11% of physicians practice in those same communities.&nbsp; We are on a mission to transform healthcare in rural America.&nbsp; We provide high-quality primary care tailored to meet the needs of our patients through our robust care model and comprehensive care team, delivering care in our clinics, and across settings, and wrapping resources around the patients who need them most.&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;</p> <p>Our patients and the care teams who serve them sit at the center of everything we do at Hopscotch.&nbsp; Hopscotch Primary Care takes a team approach to serve patient needs and provide the best care possible.&nbsp; Our goal is to provide the care each of us would want for ourselves or for our family members, in the right setting, and at the right time.&nbsp;</p> <p>Today, we are serving thousands of patients in our value-based care model and the number is growing every day.&nbsp; If you want to bring your experience, skill and passion to make a lasting impact in healthcare, we’d like to meet you.&nbsp;</p></div><p>&nbsp;</p> <h3>ABOUT THE ROLE</h3> <p>We are looking for a&nbsp;<strong>Senior Data Engineer&nbsp;</strong>(remote) to own and evolve the backbone of our data platform. This role is for a hands-on builder who thrives at the intersection of data engineering, software architecture, and pragmatic problem solving. You’ll design and scale the systems that ensure our data is trustworthy, accessible, and ready to power everything from day-to-day operations to next-generation AI applications. While this is not a people manager role, you will serve as a technical leader on the team, raising the bar on engineering fundamentals and helping shape the long-term direction of our data ecosystem. You will be part of a skilled, passionate, and collaborative team committed to working together to realize Hopscotch’s ambitious and important vision.&nbsp;&nbsp;</p> <h3>RESPONSIBILITIES &nbsp;</h3> <ul> <li> <ul> <li><strong>Architect, build, and maintain</strong> our data model, data pipelines, and data access layer that support our care delivery, operations, and analytics in collaboration with the data platform team.&nbsp;&nbsp;</li> <li><strong>Own the end-to-end lifecycle&nbsp;</strong>of data pipelines that enable key business operations, support third-party integrations, and ensure timely, accurate data delivery across internal and external platforms.</li> <li><strong>Ensure data quality &amp; governance</strong>: implement practices that make our data trustworthy and usable for all stakeholders, from clinicians to operators.&nbsp;&nbsp;</li> <li><strong>Monitor and optimize data infrastructure</strong>: troubleshoot issues, improve system performance, and drive efficiency through automation and process enhancements.&nbsp;&nbsp;</li> <li><strong>Lead with technical depth</strong>: review code and set engineering best practices across the team.&nbsp;&nbsp;</li> <li><strong>Think big picture</strong>: design today’s infrastructure with tomorrow’s needs in mind, laying the foundation for high quality workflow enhancements as well as advanced analytics and AI.</li> <li><strong>Contribute where business needs are highest:</strong> flex across the stack, from backend data engineering to occasional contributions in frontend applications that bring data to life</li> <li><strong>Collaborate with cross-functional teams</strong>: work with product managers, analytics, operations, and care delivery stakeholders to translate business needs into scalable data solutions.&nbsp;&nbsp;</li> </ul> </li> </ul> <h3>ABOUT YOU</h3> <p>You would be a great fit for this position if you:&nbsp;</p> <ul> <li>Have an ownership mentality; take high-level business and technical challenges, frame them into actionable work, and deliver solutions with minimal oversight.</li> <li>Are customer-focused with a passion for serving patients and providers who make healthcare possible</li> <li>Are results-driven, focusing time and resources against the most important priorities</li> <li>Comfortable with ambiguity and bring a structured, proactive approach to executing</li> <li>Energized by building relationships and finding creative solutions</li> <li>Articulate and succinct with communication about complex concepts, both verbally and in writing</li> </ul> <p>From a cultural perspective, you are:</p> <ul> <li>Agile, yet thoughtful, and can work effectively in a fast-paced, ever-changing environment · Constantly seeking ways to simplify and improve how things are done, bringing a positive attitude to challenges</li> <li>Always looking for ways to modernize our stack, testing out the limits of new technologies, and applying new technologies to solve problems where applicable</li> <li>Accountable, holding yourself, and others, to a high standard</li> <li>Supportive of teammates and aim to share knowledge with others</li> <li>Willing to roll up your sleeves to get work done and collaborate effectively with people of all backgrounds</li> </ul> <h3>ADDITIONAL QUALIFICATIONS</h3> <ul> <li>Bachelor’s degree in computer science, engineering, or related field</li> <li>4+ years of professional experience in data engineering and software architecture shipping production level code, preferably in healthcare services</li> <li>Strong programming skills (Python, Pyspark, or similar) with proven experience in building and scaling reusable, testable, and maintainable data pipelines</li> <li>Experience designing and optimizing data models, warehouses, and/or lakehouses</li> <li>Familiarity with modern data platforms and cloud ecosystems (ex. Palantir Foundry, DBT, Databricks, Snowflake, AWS/GCP/Azure).</li> <li>Track record of ensuring data quality, governance, and reliability</li> <li>Ability to balance high-level architecture with tactical execution</li> </ul> <p class="x_MsoNormal"><span data-olk-copy-source="MessageBody">In accordance with the Illinois Pay Transparency Act, we’re providing the full salary range for this position. Actual compensation will be determined based on location, relevant experience and qualifications. Most offers will fall within a portion of the listed range. </span>This position has a salary range of <span data-olk-copy-source="MessageBody">$140,000-170,000 </span>annually,<em> </em>based on location, relevant experience and qualifications. In addition to base salary, we offer a comprehensive benefits package that includes:</p> <ul type="disc"> <li class="x_MsoNormal">Paid holidays + PTO</li> <li class="x_MsoNormal">Company sponsored medical, dental, and vision insurance for you + your family</li> <li class="x_MsoNormal">FREE short-term and long-term disability insurance</li> <li class="x_MsoNormal">FREE $100k life insurance policy</li> <li class="x_MsoNormal">401k plan with 4% company match + no vesting period</li> <li class="x_MsoNormal">$720 - $1,000 added to employee Health Savings Account annually for eligible health plans</li> <li class="x_MsoNormal">And more!</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><span data-ccp-props="{&quot;134233117&quot;:true,&quot;134233118&quot;:true,&quot;201341983&quot;:2,&quot;335557856&quot;:16777215,&quot;335559740&quot;:300}">&nbsp;</span></p><div class="content-conclusion"><p><em>At Hopscotch Primary Care, we embrace diversity, invest in a culture of inclusion and positivity and encourage all to apply to join our team.&nbsp; You will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.&nbsp;</em></p></div>

title: Senior Data Engineer
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><h2><span data-ccp-props="{&quot;134233117&quot;:true,&quot;134233118&quot;:true,&quot;201341983&quot;:0,&quot;335559740&quot;:240}">About Hopscotch Primary Care</span></h2> <p>At Hopscotch Primary Care, we believe great healthcare should be accessible to all people across all communities.&nbsp; Today, almost 20% of Americans live in a rural community, yet only 11% of physicians practice in those same communities.&nbsp; We are on a mission to transform healthcare in rural America.&nbsp; We provide high-quality primary care tailored to meet the needs of our patients through our robust care model and comprehensive care team, delivering care in our clinics, and across settings, and wrapping resources around the patients who need them most.&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;</p> <p>Our patients and the care teams who serve them sit at the center of everything we do at Hopscotch.&nbsp; Hopscotch Primary Care takes a team approach to serve patient needs and provide the best care possible.&nbsp; Our goal is to provide the care each of us would want for ourselves or for our family members, in the right setting, and at the right time.&nbsp;</p> <p>Today, we are serving thousands of patients in our value-based care model and the number is growing every day.&nbsp; If you want to bring your experience, skill and passion to make a lasting impact in healthcare, we’d like to meet you.&nbsp;</p></div><p>&nbsp;</p> <h3>ABOUT THE ROLE</h3> <p>We are looking for a Senior Data Engineer (remote) to own and evolve the backbone of our data platform. title: Senior Data Engineer
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p><span style="font-family: verdana, geneva, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><strong><span data-contrast="none">Who We Are</span></strong></span></p> <p><span style="font-family: verdana, geneva, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Imagine Pediatrics is a tech enabled, pediatrician led medical group reimagining care for children with special health care needs. We deliver 24/7 virtual first and in home medical, behavioral, and social care, working alongside families, providers, and health plans to break down barriers to quality care. We do not replace existing care teams; we enhance them, providing an extra layer of support with compassion, creativity, and an unwavering commitment to children with medical complexity.</span></p></div><h2><span style="font-family: verdana, geneva, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">What You'll Do</span></h2> <p><span style="font-family: verdana, geneva, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">As a Senior Data Engineer at Imagine Pediatrics, you will design, build, refactor, and maintain the data models and pipelines that power product, clinical analytics, and operational reporting across the organization. This role requires a mix of technical expertise and analytical thinking, applying engineering best practices to transform raw data from a variety of sources into well-defined, consumable datasets and making them accessible in multiple systems. You will:</span></p> <ul> <li style="font-family: verdana, geneva, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-family: verdana, geneva, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Design and build scalable, well-tested DBT models.</span></li> <li style="font-family: verdana, geneva, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-family: verdana, geneva, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Develop and maintain scalable ELT pipelines, ensuring observability and reliability across workflows while leveraging multiple languages and frameworks.</span></li> <li style="font-family: verdana, geneva, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-family: verdana, geneva, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Write performance-aware SQL, understand query execution, and identify and resolve bottlenecks in queries and pipelines.</span></li> <li style="font-family: verdana, geneva, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-family: verdana, geneva, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Improve existing patterns in the codebase and establish new ones.</span></li> <li style="font-family: verdana, geneva, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-family: verdana, geneva, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Partner with product managers, engineers, and stakeholders to understand data needs and provide actionable paths forward. Work closely with engineers across teams to resolve dependencies, facilitate alignment on technical decisions, and mentor other engineers.</span></li> <li style="font-family: verdana, geneva, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-family: verdana, geneva, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Document logic, business rules, and methodology to promote transparency and enable teammates to reference and build on your work.</span></li> </ul> <h2><span style="font-family: verdana, geneva, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">What You Bring &amp; How You'll Qualify</span></h2> <p><span style="font-family: verdana, geneva, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">You are driven by curiosity and committed to reimagining pediatric healthcare and creating a world where every child with complex medical conditions gets the care and support, they deserve. You thrive in a fast-paced startup environment, embrace change as an opportunity for growth, and bring both engineering depth and a collaborative mindset. You are proactive, identify gaps in your own skillset and take action to improve, and aren't afraid to push back on unclear assumptions.</span></p> <h3><span style="font-family: verdana, geneva, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><em>Required</em></span></h3> <ul> <li style="font-family: verdana, geneva, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-family: verdana, geneva, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">5–7+ years of healthcare data engineering or analytics engineering experience, with expert-level SQL proficiency and strong data modeling fundamentals.</span></li> <li style="font-family: verdana, geneva, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-family: verdana, geneva, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Strong experience with dbt and orchestration tools.</span></li> <li style="font-family: verdana, geneva, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><span style="font-family: verdana, geneva, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Experience with cloud data warehouses, preferably Snowflake, including performance-aware SQL development and query optimization.</span></li> <li style="font-family: verdana, geneva, sans-serif; full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p><span style="font-family: verdana, geneva, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;"><strong><span data-contrast="none">Who We Are</span></strong></span></p> <p><span style="font-family: verdana, geneva, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Imagine Pediatrics is a tech enabled, pediatrician led medical group reimagining care for children with special health care needs. We deliver 24/7 virtual first and in home medical, behavioral, and social care, working alongside families, providers, and health plans to break down barriers to quality care. We do not replace existing care teams; we enhance them, providing an extra layer of support with compassion, creativity, and an unwavering commitment to children with medical complexity.</span></p></div><p><span style="font-family: verdana, geneva, sans-serif;
