full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p><strong><span data-contrast="none">About SRS Acquiom&nbsp;&nbsp;</span></strong><span data-contrast="none">&nbsp;</span><span data-ccp-props="{&quot;134233117&quot;:false,&quot;134233118&quot;:false,&quot;335557856&quot;:16777215,&quot;335559738&quot;:240,&quot;335559739&quot;:240}">&nbsp;</span></p> <p>SRS Acquiom delivers the smartest way to run a deal™ through a platform and services designed to help deal parties manage complex M&amp;A and loan agency transactions more efficiently.</p> <p>Based in Denver, Colorado, with offices across the United States and in London and Amsterdam,&nbsp;SRS Acquiom supports transactions across North America and Europe. Our M&amp;A services include professional shareholder representation, paying and escrow agent services, and online document solicitation and reporting. For loan and credit transactions, we provide independent Administrative Agent, Collateral Agent, Sub-Agent, and Successor Agent services.</p> <p>Since 2007, SRS Acquiom has supported more than 11,500 transactions globally. By bringing efficiency, expertise, and purpose-built technology to complex financial transactions, we help sophisticated deal parties focus on building great businesses and maximizing value.</p> Summary</span></strong></p> <p>We are growing our Platform Engineering team and are looking to add a Data Engineer who will be able to help build our internal data platform. This data platform is central to ensuring that our software development and data analytics teams have governed access to the data they need. As a Data Engineer on the Platform Team, you will be a part of driving the design, development, and implementation of our data platforms. Your responsibilities include partnering with the architect and engineering teams in developing our platform, as well as providing best practices and approaches to ensure security, resiliency, and availability of solutions. You will be challenged to create strategies that ensure the secure consumption of the platform through continuous innovation, simplification, and self-service automation.</p> <p><strong>Location:&nbsp;</strong>This position is fully remote within the Continental United States. operational models</li> <li>Be a trusted advisor for initiatives by providing objective, practical, and relevant ideas, insights, and advice while also building organizational partnerships and networks to ensure comprehensive capabilities are developed with input from appropriate business and Engineering resources</li> <li>Directly support the use and delivery of data platform services in the organization</li> <li>Ensure that all data platform solutions follow established security and compliance controls</li> <li>Maintain existing and future software services code and Summary</span></strong></p> <p>We are growing our Platform Engineering team and are looking to add a Senior Data Engineer who will be able to help build our internal data platform. This data platform is central to ensuring that our software development and data analytics teams have governed access to the data they need. As a Senior Data Engineer on the Platform Team, you will be a part of driving the design, development, and implementation of our data platforms. Your responsibilities include partnering with the architect and engineering teams in developing our platform, as well as providing best practices and approaches to ensure security, resiliency, and availability of solutions. You will be challenged to create strategies that ensure the secure consumption of the platform through continuous innovation, simplification, and self-service automation.</p> <p><strong>Location:&nbsp;</strong>This position is fully remote within the Continental United States. In-person visits to the Denver headquarters are required, on average three times per year.&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Compensation: </strong>The salary range for this position is between $145k and $160k, depending on experience level.</p> <p><strong><span data-contrast="none">Primary Responsibilities&nbsp;&nbsp;</span></strong><span data-contrast="none">&nbsp;</span><span all initiatives keeping all compliance, security and regulatory needs satisfied</li> <li>Be a thought leader by staying abreast of current and emerging technologies and industry trends.</li> <li>Work with your peers to design and build data platform capabilities and incorporate the necessary automations and tool configurations that ensure agile delivery and secure consumption</li> <li>Design and implement processes and tools that enable product engineering and BI/Analytics teams to consume an extensible and scalable data platform, which enforces all needed governance and consistent operational models</li> <li>Be a trusted advisor for initiatives by providing objective, practical, and relevant ideas, insights, and advice while also building organizational partnerships and networks to ensure comprehensive capabilities are developed with input from appropriate business and Engineering resources</li> <li>Directly support the use and delivery of data platform services in the organization</li> <li>Ensure that all data platform solutions follow established security and compliance controls</li> <li>Maintain existing and future software services code and configuration</li> <li>Make recommendations for improvements to existing architecture</li> <li>Help implement new technologies for future deployment</li> <li>Provide technical guidance, knowledge transfers, and mentorship to clients on their data platform adoption</li> <li>Help implement and improve the development of data pipelines</li> <li>Other duties as assigned</li> </ul> <p><span data-contrast="none">&nbsp;</span><strong><span data-contrast="none">Required Qualifications &amp; Skills</span></strong><span data-contrast="none">&nbsp;</span><span Computer Science or equivalent experience, 6+ years of experience with and strong working knowledge of SQL required, and understanding of data models is preferred</li> <li>6+ years of experience with or strong knowledge of Postgresql, MySQL, or other relational database design and programming: stored procedures, functions, PL/pgSQL</li> <li>Experience with or working knowledge of managing AWS Data Infrastructure: S3, RDS, Managed Streaming Kafka, Lake Formation, Glue, Glue Data Catalog, Athena, Quicksight, DataZone</li> <li>Experience with systems engineering and/or software development: Java, Javascript, Python</li> <li>Experience with modern API platform design, security practices, and data architectures: event-driven architecture, change data capture, pub/sub</li> </ul> integrations from third-party platforms: NetSuite, Salesforce, Workday, Pendo</li> <li>Experience with reporting and analytic tools: Grafana, Kibana, Elasticsearch, Open Telemetry</li> <li>Experience with ETL processes, projects, and tooling: Boomi</li> <li>Understanding of systems hardening and secure systems configuration</li> <li>Experience with regulatory compliance standards: HIPAA, PCI, SEC, title: Staff Data Engineer
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p><strong>Exactera </strong>has offices in<strong> New York City, Tarrytown NY, San Diego, CA, London,&nbsp;</strong>and <strong>Argentina.&nbsp;</strong></p></div><p>&nbsp;</p> <h3><strong>The Role</strong></h3> <p>As Staff Data Engineer, you will provide senior onshore technical leadership for the data engineering team. You will own a defined slice of our centralized Databricks data platform with full accountability for decisions and delivery, serve as a technical counterpart to the Principal Data Platform Engineer, and drive architectural judgment and independent problem-solving as platform complexity scales post-migration.</p> <p>This is a hands-on data engineering role focused on building and maintaining production pipelines, exercising architectural judgment on data modeling and pipeline design, and serving as the onshore escalation point and institutional knowledge backup for platform decisions.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <h3><strong>The Business Challenge</strong></h3> <p>We operate multiple product lines (Transfer Pricing, R&amp;D Services, RoyaltyStat, Provisioning), each with distinct databases containing enterprise financial data—journal entries, general ledgers, and financial statements. Our immediate challenge is migrating multi-terabyte datasets from legacy systems to a unified Databricks lakehouse while establishing governance patterns that enable multi-product operations at scale. As the platform matures, the data engineering team needs senior onshore technical presence to drive architecture ownership and maintain platform quality.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <h3><strong>What You'll Build</strong></h3> <ul> <li>Production Data Pipelines: Build and maintain production data pipelines within the patterns and governance established by the Lead Data Platform Engineer, ensuring reliability and performance at multi-terabyte scale.</li> <li>Data Modeling &amp; Architecture: Exercise architectural judgment on data modeling, pipeline design, and platform usage—translating complex business requirements into scalable data solutions across our product portfolio.</li> <li>Stakeholder Engagement: Engage proactively with product and engineering stakeholders to translate requirements into data solutions, serving as the primary onshore technical point of contact for data engineering needs.</li> <li>Platform Quality: Drive platform quality through code reviews, testing practices, and engineering standards that ensure the team delivers reliable, maintainable data infrastructure.</li> <li>Knowledge &amp; Continuity: Serve as onshore escalation point and institutional knowledge backup for platform decisions, reducing single-point-of-failure risk and building onshore technical depth as the platform scales.</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <h3><strong>Business Problems You'll Solve</strong></h3> <ul> <li>Multi-Product Data Delivery: Implement data pipelines that serve multiple product lines (Transfer Pricing, R&amp;D Services, RoyaltyStat, Provisioning) with distinct data requirements, ensuring each product gets the data it needs reliably and on schedule.</li> <li>Legacy Migration Execution: Lead pipeline implementation for migrating multi-terabyte datasets from legacy systems to Databricks, working within the architecture defined by the Lead Data Platform Engineer.</li> <li>Onshore Technical Leadership: Provide the senior judgment layer the current nearshore team cannot—owning problems end-to-end, making independent architectural decisions, and mentoring engineers to raise the quality bar across the team.</li> <li>Cross-Team Coordination: Bridge the gap between product teams and data infrastructure, translating business requirements into data solutions and ensuring the data platform delivers on product commitments.</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <h3><strong>Required Experience</strong></h3> <p><strong>Core Data Engineering</strong></p> <ul> <li>SQL, Python, and PySpark—production pipeline implementation and performance optimization</li> <li>Databricks experience—Delta Lake, Workflows, and Databricks SQL; Unity Catalog familiarity preferred</li> <li>5+ years in data engineering with demonstrated ability to own problems end-to-end without close direction</li> <li>Experience building and maintaining ETL/ELT pipelines at scale, including error handling, monitoring, and data quality validation</li> <li>Strong data modeling skills across structured and semi-structured data sources</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Platform &amp; Infrastructure</strong></p> <ul> <li>AWS experience (S3, IAM, VPC) with ability to collaborate on infrastructure decisions</li> <li>Infrastructure-as-code experience (Terraform preferred)</li> <li>Familiarity with data governance patterns (Unity Catalog, data lineage, access controls)</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Technical Leadership</strong></p> <ul> <li>Demonstrated ability to exercise independent architectural judgment—not just ticket execution</li> <li>Experience mentoring or guiding junior and mid-level data engineers</li> <li>Strong written and verbal communication—able to document architecture decisions and engage directly with both technical and business stakeholders</li> <li>Onshore (US-based)—role requires timezone overlap, async-light communication, and direct stakeholder engagement</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Preferred But Not Required</strong></p> <ul> <li>Experience with financial data, accounting systems (NetSuite), or enterprise ERP platforms</li> <li>Background building pipelines that serve AI/ML workloads (preparing data for downstream ML consumption, RAG, and LLMs)</li> <li>Familiarity with data governance frameworks and compliance requirements for regulated industries</li> <li>Experience working alongside or transitioning from nearshore engineering teams</li> </ul><div class="content-conclusion"><h3>What We Offer:<br><em>(The following only applies to US-based positions)</em></h3> <ul> <li> <div>A collaborative team culture with opportunities for career development.&nbsp;</div> </li> <li> <div>Ample opportunities to be recognized, build valuable skills, and grow your career.&nbsp;</div> </li> <li> <div>Generous vacation policy, including paid parental leave.&nbsp;</div> </li> <li> <div>Comprehensive health plans with FSA and HSA options.&nbsp;</div> </li> <li> <div>401(k) retirement plan.&nbsp;</div> </li> <li> <div>Life and disability insurance coverage.&nbsp;</div> </li> <li> <div>Supplemental benefits like a dependent care savings plan, pet insurance, will preparation, and an employee assistance program.&nbsp;</div> </li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <h3>About Us:</h3> <div data-olk-copy-source="MessageBody">At Exactera, a FinTech SaaS start-up founded in 2016, we stand at the intersection of human and machine intelligence. Our corporate tax solutions are powered by AI and cloud-based technologies, serving customers worldwide. With over $100 million in funding from Savant Venture Fund and Insight Partners, we are poised for growth. We are committed to diversity, inclusion, and equal opportunities for all.&nbsp;</div> <p>&nbsp;</p></div>

title: Data Engineer (Databricks)
full_description: <div>At Livefront, we help companies design and build world-class digital products that command attention and inspire joy. We’ve helped household names like CVS, Samsung, General Mills, and Optum create experiences that have reached millions of people, and startups like HomeSpotter and Credly build entirely new businesses that challenge their industries’ status quo.</div> <div>&nbsp;</div> <div>We're looking for an outstanding Databricks Data Engineer to join our growing data practice — someone who builds the data and AI foundations that digital products and intelligent experiences run on.&nbsp;</div> <div> <div class="section page-centered" data-qa="job-description"> <div>&nbsp;</div> <h2><strong>Who you are</strong></h2> <p>You are a Databricks-focused Data Engineer who understands that great data platforms are only as valuable as the products, AI workflows, and experiences they enable. You bring deep, production-grade expertise across the Databricks platform and know how to connect platform capabilities to real business outcomes.</p> <p>You thrive in ambiguity and can quickly assess a client's data landscape to recommend and implement the right solutions. You understand that in consulting, your Databricks depth is most valuable when it connects platform capabilities to the products and experiences clients actually use — and you're as comfortable in a product design conversation as you are building a DLT pipeline.</p> <p>You excel at translating complex data challenges into clear technical requirements and can confidently navigate conversations with everyone from data scientists to executives. Your engineering principles are mature and grounded in real-world experience across various industries and scales.</p> <p>You have an interest in and a curiosity about data platforms and the latest advances in data technology.</p> <h2><strong>What you will be doing</strong></h2> <ul> <li>Design and build production data pipelines using Lakeflow Declarative Pipelines, Autoloader, and Structured Streaming, with end-to-end ownership of ingestion, transformation, data quality expectations, and CI/CD deployment via Declarative Automation Bundles.</li> <li>Architect and implement Lakehouse solutions on Databricks — medallion architecture, Delta Lake, Unity Catalog — tailored to the client's analytics, AI, and application needs.</li> <li>Build and maintain Databricks transformation layers — DLT pipelines, PySpark notebooks, and dbt — with data quality constraints and SLAs baked in.</li> <li>Design and maintain the data and AI foundations — Unity Catalog, Feature Store, MLflow, and Model Serving — that power production ML, agent workflows, and AI-enabled digital products.</li> <li>Collaborate with product and backend engineers to design data models, APIs, and application data contracts — ensuring the platform serves the product, not just the warehouse.</li> <li>Consult with clients to understand their data challenges, develop data strategies, and implement sustainable solutions.</li> <li>Adapt your approach based on project needs — sometimes leading data architecture discussions with clients, other times supporting internal teams with specialized data expertise.</li> <li>Work within multi-cloud environments — primarily AWS and Azure — anchoring data platform recommendations around Databricks where it fits the client's architecture and goals.</li> <li>Champion data governance through Unity Catalog — access control, lineage, data quality policies, and compliance — as a first-class part of every engagement, not an afterthought.</li> <li>Design data-to-application architectures — including Lakebase-backed services and Databricks Apps — that connect governed data to AI workflows, digital products, and user-facing experiences.</li> <li>Help build Livefront's Databricks practice — contributing to accelerators, internal enablement, certification goals, and Databricks partner go-to-market materials alongside delivery work.</li> </ul> <h2><strong>Why you should apply</strong></h2> <ul> <li>You want to work with passionate and talented people who are always looking for ways to make things better.</li> <li>You desire a work environment where respect, mutual trust, and egoless collaboration are paramount.</li> <li>You want colleagues who take their work seriously but not themselves, and who know how to let loose and have a good time.</li> <li>You like being part of a team with a reputation for excellence that gives back to the community by educating, mentoring, and sponsoring.</li> <li>You want to work on products and accounts that have outsized impact and reach.</li> <li>You believe in sweating the details, giving a damn about quality, and taking pride in going the extra mile.</li> <li>You want to help build a data practice specialization from the ground up — shaping how we go to market with Databricks, what we build as accelerators, and what it means to do this kind of work at a digital product company.</li> </ul> <h2><strong>What you bring to the table</strong></h2> <ul> <li>3-5 years of data engineering experience with at least 2 years in production Databricks environments, preferably in a consulting or client delivery context.</li> <li>Solid working knowledge of AWS and Azure cloud services relevant to Databricks deployments — storage, networking, IAM, and compute — with GCP familiarity a plus.</li> <li>Deep, production-grade Databricks expertise: Lakeflow Declarative Pipelines, Autoloader, Structured Streaming, Lakeflow Jobs, Unity Catalog (including fine-grained access control and lineage) — demonstrated through shipped production workloads, not prototypes.</li> <li>Proven experience designing Lakehouse architectures — medallion patterns, Delta Lake table design, partitioning, Z-ordering, and query optimization — at production scale.</li> <li>Hands-on experience with data pipeline testing, observability, and CI/CD for data — including unit testing, data quality frameworks, and version-controlled deployments via Git and Declarative Automation Bundles.</li> <li>Strong proficiency in SQL and Python, with the ability to write clean, performant, and maintainable code.</li> <li>Understanding of data modeling, schema design, and query optimization.</li> <li>Excellent communication skills with the ability to explain complex data concepts to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.</li> <li>Strong problem-solving skills with the ability to navigate ambiguous requirements and deliver pragmatic solutions.</li> <li>Above-average discipline and personal organization skills.</li> <li>Obvious comfort with critique and peer review in the context of an iterative development process.</li> <li>A demonstrated hunger for personal and professional growth.</li> <li>A self-evident love and care for the craft of data engineering.</li> </ul> <h2><strong>Bonus points if you…</strong></h2> <ul> <li>Have worked with real-time streaming technologies (Kafka, Kinesis, etc.).</li> <li>Have hands-on experience with alternative cloud data platforms — useful context for migrations and competitive assessments, though Databricks is our primary platform focus.</li> <li>Have experience in healthcare or fintech domains.</li> <li>Have hands-on experience with MLOps or LLMOps on Databricks — MLflow experiment tracking, model registry, Model Serving endpoints, or Vector Search for RAG pipelines.</li> <li>Have experience with Java, Go, or Scala.</li> <li>Have strong illustration skills for technical diagramming and data architecture documentation.</li> <li>Speak, write, and/or educate publicly about data engineering topics.</li> <li>Have contributed to open-source data projects.</li> <li>Hold or are actively pursuing a Databricks certification (Data Engineer Associate or Professional, or Apache Spark Developer) — we treat these as meaningful signals of platform depth, and they directly support our Databricks partner growth goals.</li> <li>Have experience with Databricks Apps, or Lakebase — early familiarity with where the Databricks platform is heading is a strong differentiator.</li> </ul> <h2>What to expect</h2> </div> <div class="section page-centered" data-qa="closing-description"> <div>When applying, please include a short note about yourself, a summary of your work experience, and a link to any public profiles you actively maintain (e.g., GitHub, LinkedIn, etc).</div> <div>&nbsp;</div> <div>Our hiring process moves quickly and consists of several stages for candidates who capture our attention with their initial submission, sometimes including but not limited to a short preliminary phone interview, a series of video interviews, and a short take-home exercise, which you'll have up to a week to complete.</div> <div>&nbsp;</div> <div>Compensation is $120,000 - $145,000</div> <div>&nbsp;</div> <h2>Additional information</h2> <div>We go out of our way to evaluate all employees and job applicants equally based on merit, competence, and qualifications. We encourage candidates from all backgrounds to apply and consider all qualified applicants. Don't worry, every application will be reviewed by a human.</div> </div> </div>

title: Data Engineer (Databricks)
full_description: <div>At Livefront, we help companies design and build world-class digital products that command attention and inspire joy. We’ve helped household names like CVS, Samsung, General Mills, and Optum create experiences that have reached millions of people, and startups like HomeSpotter and Credly build entirely new businesses that challenge their industries’ status quo.</div> <div>&nbsp;</div> <div>We're looking for an outstanding Databricks Data Engineer to join our growing data practice — someone who builds the data and AI foundations that digital products and intelligent experiences run on. This is a senior-level position with the opportunity to work remotely in<strong>&nbsp;</strong>Peru.</div> <div> <div class="section page-centered" data-qa="job-description"> <div>&nbsp;</div> <h2><strong>Who you are</strong></h2> <p>You are a Databricks-focused Data Engineer who understands that great data platforms are only as valuable as the products, AI workflows, and experiences they enable. You bring deep, production-grade expertise across the Databricks platform and know how to connect platform capabilities to real business outcomes.</p> <p>You thrive in ambiguity and can quickly assess a client's data landscape to recommend and implement the right solutions. You understand that in consulting, your Databricks depth is most valuable when it connects platform capabilities to the products and experiences clients actually use — and you're as comfortable in a product design conversation as you are building a DLT pipeline.</p> <p>You excel at translating complex data challenges into clear technical requirements and can confidently navigate conversations with everyone from data scientists to executives. Your engineering principles are mature and grounded in real-world experience across various industries and scales.</p> <p>You have an interest in and a curiosity about data platforms and the latest advances in data technology.</p> <h2><strong>What you will be doing</strong></h2> <ul> <li>Design and build production data pipelines using Lakeflow Declarative Pipelines, Autoloader, and Structured Streaming, with end-to-end ownership of ingestion, transformation, data quality expectations, and CI/CD deployment via Declarative Automation Bundles.</li> <li>Architect and implement Lakehouse solutions on Databricks — medallion architecture, Delta Lake, Unity Catalog — tailored to the client's analytics, AI, and application needs.</li> <li>Build and maintain Databricks transformation layers — DLT pipelines, PySpark notebooks, and dbt — with data quality constraints and SLAs baked in.</li> <li>Design and maintain the data and AI foundations — Unity Catalog, Feature Store, MLflow, and Model Serving — that power production ML, agent workflows, and AI-enabled digital products.</li> <li>Collaborate with product and backend engineers to design data models, APIs, and application data contracts — ensuring the platform serves the product, not just the warehouse.</li> <li>Consult with clients to understand their data challenges, develop data strategies, and implement sustainable solutions.</li> <li>Adapt your approach based on project needs — sometimes leading data architecture discussions with clients, other times supporting internal teams with specialized data expertise.</li> <li>Work within multi-cloud environments — primarily AWS and Azure — anchoring data platform recommendations around Databricks where it fits the client's architecture and goals.</li> <li>Champion data governance through Unity Catalog — access control, lineage, data quality policies, and compliance — as a first-class part of every engagement, not an afterthought.</li> <li>Design data-to-application architectures — including Lakebase-backed services and Databricks Apps — that connect governed data to AI workflows, digital products, and user-facing experiences.</li> <li>Help build Livefront's Databricks practice — contributing to accelerators, internal enablement, certification goals, and Databricks partner go-to-market materials alongside delivery work.</li> </ul> <h2><strong>Why you should apply</strong></h2> <ul> <li>You want to work with passionate and talented people who are always looking for ways to make things better.</li> <li>You desire a work environment where respect, mutual trust, and egoless collaboration are paramount.</li> <li>You want colleagues who take their work seriously but not themselves, and who know how to let loose and have a good time.</li> <li>You like being part of a team with a reputation for excellence that gives back to the community by educating, mentoring, and sponsoring.</li> <li>You want to work on products and accounts that have outsized impact and reach.</li> <li>You believe in sweating the details, giving a damn about quality, and taking pride in going the extra mile.</li> <li>You want to help build a data practice specialization from the ground up — shaping how we go to market with Databricks, what we build as accelerators, and what it means to do this kind of work at a digital product company.</li> </ul> <h2><strong>What you bring to the table</strong></h2> <ul> <li>3-5 years of data engineering experience with at least 2 years in production Databricks environments, preferably in a consulting or client delivery context.</li> <li>Solid working knowledge of AWS and Azure cloud services relevant to Databricks deployments — storage, networking, IAM, and compute — with GCP familiarity a plus.</li> <li>Deep, production-grade Databricks expertise: Lakeflow Declarative Pipelines, Autoloader, Structured Streaming, Lakeflow Jobs, Unity Catalog (including fine-grained access control and lineage) — demonstrated through shipped production workloads, not prototypes.</li> <li>Proven experience designing Lakehouse architectures — medallion patterns, Delta Lake table design, partitioning, Z-ordering, and query optimization — at production scale.</li> <li>Hands-on experience with data pipeline testing, observability, and CI/CD for data — including unit testing, data quality frameworks, and version-controlled deployments via Git and Declarative Automation Bundles.</li> <li>Strong proficiency in SQL and Python, with the ability to write clean, performant, and maintainable code.</li> <li>Understanding of data modeling, schema design, and query optimization.</li> <li>Excellent communication skills with the ability to explain complex data concepts to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.</li> <li>Strong problem-solving skills with the ability to navigate ambiguous requirements and deliver pragmatic solutions.</li> <li>Above-average discipline and personal organization skills.</li> <li>Obvious comfort with critique and peer review in the context of an iterative development process.</li> <li>A demonstrated hunger for personal and professional growth.</li> <li>A self-evident love and care for the craft of data engineering.</li> </ul> <h2><strong>Bonus points if you…</strong></h2> <ul> <li>Have worked with real-time streaming technologies (Kafka, Kinesis, etc.).</li> <li>Have hands-on experience with alternative cloud data platforms — useful context for migrations and competitive assessments, though Databricks is our primary platform focus.</li> <li>Have experience in healthcare or fintech domains.</li> <li>Have hands-on experience with MLOps or LLMOps on Databricks — MLflow experiment tracking, model registry, Model Serving endpoints, or Vector Search for RAG pipelines.</li> <li>Have experience with Java, Go, or Scala.</li> <li>Have strong illustration skills for technical diagramming and data architecture documentation.</li> <li>Speak, write, and/or educate publicly about data engineering topics.</li> <li>Have contributed to open-source data projects.</li> <li>Hold or are actively pursuing a Databricks certification (Data Engineer Associate or Professional, or Apache Spark Developer) — we treat these as meaningful signals of platform depth, and they directly support our Databricks partner growth goals.</li> <li>Have experience with Databricks Apps, or Lakebase — early familiarity with where the Databricks platform is heading is a strong differentiator.</li> </ul> <h2>What to expect</h2> </div> <div class="section page-centered" data-qa="closing-description"> <div>When applying, please include a short note about yourself, a summary of your work experience, and a link to any public profiles you actively maintain (e.g., GitHub, LinkedIn, etc).</div> <div>&nbsp;</div> <div>Our hiring process moves quickly and consists of several stages for candidates who capture our attention with their initial submission, sometimes including but not limited to a short preliminary phone interview, a series of video interviews, and a short take-home exercise, which you'll have up to a week to complete.</div> <div>&nbsp;</div> <h2>Additional information</h2> <div>We go out of our way to evaluate all employees and job applicants equally based on merit, competence, and qualifications. We encourage candidates from all backgrounds to apply and consider all qualified applicants. Don't worry, every application will be reviewed by a human.</div> </div> </div>

title: Associate Forward Deployed Product Manager
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p>Cresta unlocks the true potential of the customer experience, turning every conversation into a competitive advantage. Cresta’s unified AI platform combines conversational AI agents, real-time human agent augmentation, and comprehensive conversation intelligence to drive revenue and efficiency gains across every channel. The world’s leading companies, including United Airlines, Cox Communications, and Marriott, use Cresta to power world-class customer experiences every day.&nbsp;</p> <p>Born from the Stanford AI Lab, Cresta has raised more than $270 million from the world’s leading investors, including a16z, Greylock, and Sequoia. Cresta’s leadership includes some of the leading minds in AI today. Our CEO, <a href="https://www.linkedin.com/in/pingwu/">Ping Wu</a>, founded and led Google's Contact Center AI and Vertex AI platforms before joining Cresta to build the future of AI-driven customer experiences.</p> <p>Over the next few years, AI is going to redefine how people all over the world interact with businesses every day. Come build that future at Cresta.</p></div><div>&nbsp;</div> <p>Cresta is on a mission to turn every customer conversation into a competitive advantage. Our unified platform combines both AI and human intelligence to enable 1. automating conversations with AI Agents and 2. transform their contact centers to discover customer insights.</p> <p>Born from the Stanford AI lab, Cresta's co-founder and chairman is<a href="https://www.linkedin.com/in/sebastian-thrun-59a0b273/"> Sebastian Thrun</a>, the genius behind Google X, Waymo, and Udacity. Our leadership includes CEO,<a href="https://www.linkedin.com/in/pingwu/"> Ping Wu</a>, the co-founder of Google Contact Center AI &amp; Vertex AI platform,<a href="https://www.linkedin.com/in/tianlinshi/"> and co-founder, Tim Shi</a>, an early member of Open AI in 2017. Our investors include Andreessen Horowitz, Greylock Partners, Sequoia, and former AT&amp;T CEO John Donovan.&nbsp;</p> <p>Our valued customers include brands like Airbnb, United Airlines, Hilton, Porsche, United Healthcare, Intuit, Cox Communications, and Carmax and we’ve been recognized by Forbes, Forrestor, and Bain Consulting as one of the top private AI companies in the world.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>About the Role:</strong></p> <p>As an Associate Forward-Deployed Product Manager on the AI Agents team, you will create, deploy, and optimize AI Agents that transform our customers’ businesses and delight their customers. This customer-facing role blends product thinking, prompt engineering, data-driven insights, and project leadership with Strategic &amp; Fortune 500 companies.</p> <p>In this role, you’ll collaborate with senior Forward-deployed Product Managers and Forward-deployed Engineers (FDEs) to support the build of AI Agent products.&nbsp;</p> <p>What You’ll Do:</p> <ul> <li>Drive AI Agent deployments for customers across the lifecycle — from pre-sales to post-launch optimization</li> <li>Define AI roadmaps for the customer’s executive team by blending both quantitative analysis and qualitative insights; leverage automated conversation discovery tools in addition to traditional data analysis</li> <li>Iterate prompt &amp; context engineering to achieve customer’s use case and intended business outcomes</li> <li>Own designing and running evaluations and A/B tests of AI Agents with Forward-Deployed Engineers (FDEs) using Cresta’s agent builder and beta internal tools</li> <li>Improve our playbooks for agent development and customer deployment</li> </ul> <p>Why join as an Associate FDPM at Cresta?&nbsp;</p> <ul> <li>Differentiated career development:</li> <ul> <li>Get hands-on AI product experience building Agentic solutions using a cutting-edge tech stack</li> <li>Access to an accelerated timeline of customer-facing opportunities as an AI thought partner to executives &amp; leadership</li> </ul> <li>Rotational opportunities:</li> <ul> <li>Launching solutions for mid-market to Fortune 500 companies across different verticals, gaining breadth &amp; depth in industry knowledge and domain expertise</li> <li>Develop multiple functional strengths as a super-generalist: work on projects that give experience across multiple teams, including go-to-market, engineering, product, customer success, and more</li> </ul> <li>Investment &amp; mentorship:</li> <ul> <li>Mentorship from PMs who have been building GenAI solutions since GPT-2, as well as from Microsoft, TikTok, consulting firms, and start-ups</li> <li>Launching pad for your learning and career, we take that seriously as a company in scoping opportunities</li> </ul> </ul> <p><strong>What We’re Looking For:</strong></p> <p>You are a pragmatic, results-oriented problem solver who thrives in fast-paced environments and excels at turning ambiguity into execution.</p> <p>What you’ll bring:</p> <ul> <li>2+ years of relevant experience (e.g., Product Management, Consulting, Implementations, Customer / Sales Engineer, Revenue &amp; Sales Analyst, Investment Banking, Founder)</li> <li>Hands-on work style – curiosity with a bias for action. You deliver with urgency and care</li> <li>Comfortable working with ambiguity and learning quickly in a dynamic startup environment</li> <li>Willingness to travel</li> </ul> <p>Bonus Points:</p> <ul> <li>Previous exposure via clients or projects to the travel &amp; hospitality, healthcare, insurance, and financial industry domains</li> <li>Experience in a startup or high-growth SaaS environment</li> <li>Experience working closely with AI or LLM-first products</li> <li>Technical understanding, systems thinking and exposure to building Agents (not necessarily conversational, personal projects count!)</li> </ul> <p><strong>Perks &amp; Benefits:</strong></p> <p>We offer a comprehensive and people-first benefits package to support you at work and in life:</p> <ul> <li>Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision coverage with plans to fit you and your family</li> <li>Flexible PTO to take the time you need, when you need it</li> <li>Paid parental leave for all new parents welcoming a new child</li> <li>Retirement savings plan to help you plan for the future</li> <li>Remote work setup budget to help you create a productive home office</li> <li>Monthly wellness and communication stipend to keep you connected and balanced</li> <li>In-office meal program and commuter benefits provided for onsite employees</li> </ul> <p><strong>Compensation at Cresta&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Cresta’s approach to compensation is simple: recognize impact, reward excellence, and invest in our people. We offer competitive, location-based pay that reflects the market and what each individual brings to the table.</p> <p>The posted base salary range represents what we expect to pay for this role in a given location. Final offers are shaped by factors like experience, skills, education, and geography. In addition to base pay, total compensation includes equity and a comprehensive benefits package for you and your family.</p> <p><strong>OTE Range</strong>: $130,000–$170,000 + Offers Equity</p> <p>We have noticed a rise in recruiting impersonations across the industry, where scammers attempt to access candidates' personal and financial information through fake interviews and offers. All Cresta recruiting email communications will always come from the @cresta.ai domain. Any outreach claiming to be from Cresta via other sources should be ignored.&nbsp; If you are uncertain whether you have been contacted by an official Cresta employee, reach out to recruiting@cresta.ai</p>

The future of work is here, and it's at Cresta.</p> <h4>About the role:</h4> <p>At Cresta, the AI Agent team is on a mission to create state-of-the-art AI Agents that solve practical problems for our customers. data-end="1395">Support performance tuning and troubleshooting of AI agent prototypes.</li> <li data-start="1278" data-end="1395">Work with customers and internal engineering teams to understand requirements and iterate on solutions.</li> <li data-start="1278" data-end="1395">Participate in team demos, feedback sessions, and learning opportunities.</li> <li data-start="1278" data-end="1395">This role provides mentorship and exposure to customer-facing technical problem solving in a fast-moving AI/Product environment.</li> </ul> <h4><strong>What We're Looking For:</strong></h4> <ul> <li>Enrolled in a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree program in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.</li> <li>Experience writing code in&nbsp;<strong data-start="2375" data-end="2385">Python</strong> (or another general-purpose language).</li> <li>Basic understanding of programming fundamentals and software development best practices.</li> <li>Strong interest in artificial intelligence, machine learning, or software engineering.</li> <li>Excellent communication skills and willingness to collaborate with teammates and customers.</li> </ul> <p data-start="2862" data-end="2879"><strong data-start="2862" data-end="2879">Nice-to-Haves</strong></p> <ul> <li data-start="2882" data-end="2982">Experience with APIs, web development, or cloud platforms.</li> <li data-start="2882" data-end="2982">Prior coursework or projects involving AI/ML concepts or LLMs.</li> <li data-start="2882" data-end="2982">Exposure to prompt engineering or similar experimentation.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Compensation:&nbsp;</strong>CAD $30-50/hour<br><strong>Perks:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Lunch and dinner can be expensed (up to $25 each) while working in the office</li> <li>Well-stocked office with snacks and drinks</li> <li>Convenient downtown location near Union Station (public transit accessible)</li> <li>PTO: 4 days</li> <li>Exposure to cutting-edge AI systems in production</li> </ul> <p data-pm-slice="1 1 []">This posting will be used to fill a newly-created role.</p> <p>We have noticed a rise in recruiting impersonations across the industry, where scammers attempt to access candidates' personal and financial information through fake interviews and offers. All Cresta recruiting email communications will always come from the @cresta.ai domain.&nbsp;Any outreach claiming to be from Cresta via other sources should be ignored.&nbsp; If you are uncertain whether you have been contacted by an official Cresta employee, reach out to <a href="mailto:recruiting@cresra.ai">recruiting@cresta.ai</a></p>

You anticipate needs and bottlenecks and deliver with urgency and care.</li> <li>Experience leading complex deployments, owning customer relationships (including senior customer stakeholders), and working cross-functionally</li> <li>Strong technical understanding, systems thinking and exposure to building Agents (not necessarily conversational, personal projects count!)</li> <li>Comfortable working with ambiguity and learning quickly in a dynamic startup environment</li> <li>Passion for AI, automation, and shaping the future of customer experience</li> </ul> <p><strong>Bonus Points:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Background in product management, conversation design or forward deployed engineering roles</li> <li>Experience in a startup or high-growth SaaS environment</li> <li>Familiarity with customer support and contact center operations</li> </ul> <p><strong>Perks &amp; Benefits:</strong></p> <p>We offer a comprehensive and people-first benefits package to support you at work and in life:</p> <ul> <li>Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision coverage with plans to fit you and your family</li> <li>Flexible PTO to take the time you need, when you need it</li> <li>Paid parental leave for all new parents welcoming a new child</li> <li>Retirement savings plan to help you plan for the future</li> <li>Remote work setup budget to help you create a productive home office</li> <li>Monthly wellness and communication stipend to keep you connected and balanced</li> <li>In-office meal program and commuter benefits provided for onsite employees</li> </ul> <p><strong>Compensation at Cresta&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Cresta’s approach to compensation is simple: recognize impact, reward excellence, and invest in our people. As the first hire in EMEA, you’ll also play a key role in setting the standards for the role in the EMEA region and helping to grow the team as we scale.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do</strong></p> <ul> <li><strong>Own AI Agent outcomes for our customers</strong> — defining and delivering on the metrics that move the needle. This includes finding additional automation opportunities that can provide even more value! To do this, you’ll…<br><br></li> <li><strong>Own the deployment lifecycle</strong> for AI Agents — from scoping and kickoff to launch and post-go-live optimization<br><br></li> <li><strong>Design, build, test and iterate on AI agents</strong> in partnership with FDEs using Cresta’s internal tools and the latest technologies to deliver the highest customer ROI<br><br></li> <li><strong>Serve as the AI Agent SME for the EMEA Sales cycle,</strong> partnering on demos, solutioning, and deal reviews to ensure technical feasibility, value articulation, and successful handoff to deployment.<br><br></li> <li><strong>Lead customer relationships</strong> and act as the primary point of contact for AI Agent. Help customers move faster to ensure we can deliver quickly and effectively.<br><br></li> <li><strong>Facilitate workshops and design sessions</strong> with customer stakeholders to align on agent use cases, workflows, and success criteria<br><br></li> <li><strong>Work closely with FDEs</strong> to ensure smooth implementation, build new capabilities as needed and provide feedback and improvements to our broader platform<br><br></li> <li><strong>Improve our playbooks </strong>for agent development and customer deployment<br><br></li> <li><strong>Build our forward deployed practice </strong>for EMEA, setting the standards for future growth<br><br></li> </ul> <p><strong>What We’re Looking For</strong></p> <ul> <li>5+ years of experience in Technology Consulting, Implementations, Product, or Customer Success roles, ideally working closely with AI or LLM first products<br><br></li> <li>Hands-on work style – curiosity with a bias for action. You anticipate needs and bottlenecks and deliver with urgency and care.<br><br></li> <li>Experience leading complex deployments, owning customer relationships (including senior customer stakeholders), and working cross-functionally<br><br></li> <li>Strong technical understanding, systems thinking and exposure to building Agents (not necessarily conversational, personal projects count!)<br><br></li> <li>Comfortable working with ambiguity and learning quickly in a dynamic startup environment<br><br></li> <li>Passion for AI, automation, and shaping the future of customer experience<br><br></li> </ul> <p><strong>Bonus Points</strong></p> <ul> <li>Background in product management, conversation design or forward deployed engineering roles<br><br></li> <li>Experience in a startup or high-growth SaaS environment<br><br></li> <li>Familiarity with customer support and contact center operations</li> </ul> <p><strong>Perks &amp; Benefits:</strong></p> <p>We offer a comprehensive and people-first benefits package to support you at work and in life:</p> <ul> <li>Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision coverage with plans to fit you and your family</li> <li>Paid parental leave for all new parents welcoming a new child</li> <li>Remote work setup budget to help you create a productive home office</li> <li>Monthly wellness and communication stipend to keep you connected and balanced</li> <li>20 days of vacation time&nbsp;</li> </ul> <p><strong>Compensation at Cresta&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Cresta’s approach to compensation is simple: recognize impact, reward excellence, and invest in our people. We offer competitive, location-based pay that reflects the market and what each individual brings to the table.</p> <p>Final offers are shaped by factors like experience, skills, education, and geography. In addition to base pay, total compensation includes equity and a comprehensive benefits package for you and your family.</p> <p>We have noticed a rise in recruiting impersonations across the industry, where scammers attempt to access candidates' personal and financial information through fake interviews and offers. All Cresta recruiting email communications will always come from the @cresta.ai domain. Any outreach claiming to be from Cresta via other sources should be ignored.&nbsp; If you are uncertain whether you have been contacted by an official Cresta employee, reach out to recruiting@cresta.ai</p>

title: Senior Forward Deployed Engineer (AI Agent)
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p>Cresta unlocks the true potential of the customer experience, turning every conversation into a competitive advantage. Cresta’s unified AI platform combines conversational AI agents, real-time human agent augmentation, and comprehensive conversation intelligence to drive revenue and efficiency gains across every channel. The world’s leading companies, including United Airlines, Cox Communications, and Marriott, use Cresta to power world-class customer experiences every day.&nbsp;</p> <p>Born from the Stanford AI Lab, Cresta has raised more than $270 million from the world’s leading investors, including a16z, Greylock, and Sequoia. Cresta’s leadership includes some of the leading minds in AI today. Our CEO, <a href="https://www.linkedin.com/in/pingwu/">Ping Wu</a>, founded and led Google's Contact Center AI and Vertex AI platforms before joining Cresta to build the future of AI-driven customer experiences.</p> <p>Over the next few years, AI is going to redefine how people all over the world interact with businesses every day. Come build that future at Cresta.</p></div><h4>About the role:</h4> <p>At Cresta, the AI Agent team is on a mission to create state-of-the-art AI Agents that solve practical problems for our customers. We are focused on leveraging the latest technologies in Large Language Models (LLMs) and AI Agent systems, while ensuring that the solutions we develop are cost-effective, secure, and reliable.&nbsp;</p> <p>As an <strong>AI Agent Engineer</strong>, you'll be at the forefront of deploying AI agents that address real-world challenges. In this role, you will work closely with customers as well as our software and machine learning engineers, ensuring high-impact AI Agent deployments and contributing to the continuous improvement of our core AI platform. You’ll develop intelligent AI agents, integrate them seamlessly with external systems and offer hands-on technical expertise to ensure successful deployments.. This position requires strong engineering skills, adaptability, and customer engagement. If you are self-driven, analytical, and eager to leverage AI in practical applications, this role is for you.</p> <p><strong>What You'll Do </strong>:</p> <ul> <li><strong>AI Agent Development:</strong> Develop, configure, deploy, and optimize AI agents using Cresta’s AI platform and tools.</li> <li><strong>System Integration:</strong> Build AI agent integrations with external systems (APIs, databases, CRMs, etc.) to ensure seamless workflow integration.</li> <li><strong>Performance Optimization:</strong> Optimize AI agent performance (e.g. fine-tune prompts and configurations) and troubleshoot issues in complex enterprise environments.</li> <li><strong>Requirements Gathering:</strong> Collaborate with customers and internal stakeholders to gather technical requirements and translate business needs into AI Agent solutions.</li> <li><strong>Demos &amp; Iteration:</strong> Conduct interactive demos and present compelling proof-of-concepts to prospective customers, proactively gather feedback, and iteratively refine solutions to meet objectives.</li> <li><strong>Project Coordination:</strong> Define project milestones, create implementation plans, and coordinate execution with internal teams to ensure on-time delivery. Provide a tight feedback loop to our product and engineering teams — identifying gaps, building custom tooling, and influencing the roadmap through real-world deployment learnings.</li> <li><strong>Partner Cross-Functionally:</strong> Collaborate with PMs to define agent goals, iterate rapidly based on customer feedback, and shape product capabilities that maximize customer ROI.</li> <li><strong>Technical Guidance:</strong> Serve as a trusted technical advisor for the customer, guiding best practices for AI agent adoption and usage. Provide technical guidance on AI agent best practices, including architecture design, security considerations, and scalability planning.</li> </ul> <h4><strong>What We're Looking For:</strong></h4> <ul> <li><strong>Education: </strong>Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.</li> <li><strong>Experience: </strong>3+ years of full-time working experience in software development/Consulting, AI/ML engineering, or Forward Deployed Engineering.</li> <li><strong>Programming Skills:</strong> Proficiency in Python and Golang, with the ability to write clean, efficient code.</li> <li><strong>AI/ML Knowledge: </strong>Familiarity with AI/ML concepts. Hands-on experience with large language models (LLMs), and prompt engineering techniques are strongly preferred.</li> <li><strong>AI Agent Frameworks: </strong>Strong understanding of general AI agent frameworks, function calling, and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG). Hands-on experience of building such a system is strongly preferred.</li> <li><strong>Cloud &amp; DevOps: </strong>Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, or Azure) and DevOps practices (CI/CD, containerization, monitoring).</li> <li><strong>Integration Expertise: </strong>Hands-on experience with integrating systems via APIs, webhooks, and data pipelines.</li> <li><strong>Communication:</strong> Excellent communication and project management skills.</li> <li><strong>Analytical Approach: </strong>Ability to use data-driven decision-making, including A/B testing and performance monitoring, to refine solutions.</li> <li><strong>Collaborative Builder:</strong> You thrive in cross-functional environments, working hand-in-hand with PMs and engineers to turn real customer problems into scalable AI solutions.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Note on Our Hiring Process</strong></p> <p>We highly value your time and engineering craftsmanship. To ensure a streamlined and objective process, the first step for qualified applicants following an initial resume review is a self-paced CoderPad technical assessment. This allows you to showcase your practical coding and problem-solving skills in a real-world environment before moving into conversational interview rounds.</p> <p><strong>Perks &amp; Benefits:</strong></p> <p>We offer a comprehensive and people-first benefits package to support you at work and in life:</p> <ul> <li>Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision coverage with plans to fit you and your family</li> <li>Flexible PTO to take the time you need, when you need it</li> <li>Paid parental leave for all new parents welcoming a new child</li> <li>Retirement savings plan to help you plan for the future</li> <li>Remote work setup budget to help you create a productive home office</li> <li>Monthly wellness and communication stipend to keep you connected and balanced</li> <li>In-office meal program and commuter benefits provided for onsite employees</li> </ul> <p><strong>Compensation at Cresta:&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Cresta’s approach to compensation is simple: recognize impact, reward excellence, and invest in our people. We offer competitive, location-based pay that reflects the market and what each individual brings to the table.</p> <p>The posted base salary range represents what we expect to pay for this role in a given location. Final offers are shaped by factors like experience, skills, education, and geography. In addition to base pay, total compensation includes equity and a comprehensive benefits package for you and your family.</p> <p><strong>Base Salary Range</strong>: $185,000–$235,000 + Annual Bonus &amp; Offers Equity</p> <p>We have noticed a rise in recruiting impersonations across the industry, where scammers attempt to access candidates' personal and financial information through fake interviews and offers. All Cresta recruiting email communications will always come from the @cresta.ai domain. Any outreach claiming to be from Cresta via other sources should be ignored.&nbsp; If you are uncertain whether you have been contacted by an official Cresta employee, reach out to&nbsp;<a href="mailto:recruiting@cresta.ai">recruiting@cresta.ai</a>&nbsp;</p>

Your recruiter can provide further details.</p> <p data-pm-slice="1 1 []">This posting will be used to fill a newly-created role.</p> <p>We have noticed a rise in recruiting impersonations across the industry, where scammers attempt to access candidates' personal and financial information through fake interviews and offers. title: Senior Forward Deployed Engineer (AI Agent) - Germany
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p>Cresta unlocks the true potential of the customer experience, turning every conversation into a competitive advantage. Cresta’s unified AI platform combines conversational AI agents, real-time human agent augmentation, and comprehensive conversation intelligence to drive revenue and efficiency gains across every channel. The world’s leading companies, including United Airlines, Cox Communications, and Marriott, use Cresta to power world-class customer experiences every day.&nbsp;</p> <p>Born from the Stanford AI Lab, Cresta has raised more than $270 million from the world’s leading investors, including a16z, Greylock, and Sequoia. Cresta’s leadership includes some of the leading minds in AI today. Our CEO, <a href="https://www.linkedin.com/in/pingwu/">Ping Wu</a>, founded and led Google's Contact Center AI and Vertex AI platforms before joining Cresta to build the future of AI-driven customer experiences.</p> <p>Over the next few years, AI is going to redefine how people all over the world interact with businesses every day. Come build that future at Cresta.</p></div><h4>About the role:</h4> <p>At Cresta, the AI Agent team is on a mission to create state-of-the-art AI Agents that solve practical problems for our customers. We are focused on leveraging the latest technologies in Large Language Models (LLMs) and AI Agent systems, while ensuring that the solutions we develop are cost-effective, secure, and reliable.&nbsp;</p> <p>As an <strong>AI Agent Engineer</strong>, you'll be at the forefront of deploying AI agents that address real-world challenges. In this role, you will work closely with customers as well as our software and machine learning engineers, ensuring high-impact AI Agent deployments and contributing to the continuous improvement of our core AI platform. You’ll develop intelligent AI agents, integrate them seamlessly with external systems and offer hands-on technical expertise to ensure successful deployments.. This position requires strong engineering skills, adaptability, and customer engagement. If you are self-driven, analytical, and eager to leverage AI in practical applications, this role is for you.</p> <p><strong>What You'll Do </strong>:</p> <ul> <li><strong>AI Agent Development:</strong> Develop, configure, deploy, and optimize AI agents using Cresta’s AI platform and tools.</li> <li><strong>System Integration:</strong> Build AI agent integrations with external systems (APIs, databases, CRMs, etc.) to ensure seamless workflow integration.</li> <li><strong>Performance Optimization:</strong> Optimize AI agent performance (e.g. fine-tune prompts and configurations) and troubleshoot issues in complex enterprise environments.</li> <li><strong>Requirements Gathering:</strong> Collaborate with customers and internal stakeholders to gather technical requirements and translate business needs into AI Agent solutions.</li> <li><strong>Demos &amp; Iteration:</strong> Conduct interactive demos and present compelling proof-of-concepts to prospective customers, proactively gather feedback, and iteratively refine solutions to meet objectives.</li> <li><strong>Project Coordination:</strong> Define project milestones, create implementation plans, and coordinate execution with internal teams to ensure on-time delivery. Provide a tight feedback loop to our product and engineering teams — identifying gaps, building custom tooling, and influencing the roadmap through real-world deployment learnings.</li> <li><strong>Partner Cross-Functionally:</strong> Collaborate with PMs to define agent goals, iterate rapidly based on customer feedback, and shape product capabilities that maximize customer ROI.</li> <li><strong>Technical Guidance:</strong> Serve as a trusted technical advisor for the customer, guiding best practices for AI agent adoption and usage. Provide technical guidance on AI agent best practices, including architecture design, security considerations, and scalability planning.</li> </ul> <h4><strong>What We're Looking For:</strong></h4> <ul> <li><strong>Education: </strong>Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.</li> <li><strong>Experience: </strong>3+ years of experience of full time working experience in software development/ consulting, AI/ML engineering, or system integration, or as FDE</li> <li><strong>Programming Skills:</strong> Proficiency in Python and Golang, with the ability to write clean, efficient code.</li> <li><strong>AI/ML Knowledge: </strong>Familiarity with AI/ML concepts. Hands-on experience with large language models (LLMs), and prompt engineering techniques are strongly preferred.</li> <li><strong>AI Agent Frameworks: </strong>Strong understanding of general AI agent frameworks, function calling, and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG). Hands-on experience of building such a system is strongly preferred.</li> <li><strong>Cloud &amp; DevOps: </strong>Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, or Azure) and DevOps practices (CI/CD, containerization, monitoring).</li> <li><strong>Integration Expertise: </strong>Hands-on experience with integrating systems via APIs, webhooks, and data pipelines.</li> <li><strong>Communication:</strong> Excellent communication and project management skills.</li> <li><strong>Analytical Approach: </strong>Ability to use data-driven decision-making, including A/B testing and performance monitoring, to refine solutions.</li> <li><strong>Collaborative Builder:</strong> You thrive in cross-functional environments, working hand-in-hand with PMs and engineers to turn real customer problems into scalable AI solutions.</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>Compensation for this position includes a base salary, equity, and a variety of benefits. Actual base salaries will be based on candidate-specific factors, including experience, skillset, and location, and local minimum pay requirements as applicable.</p> <p data-pm-slice="1 1 []">This posting will be used to fill a newly-created role.</p> <p>We have noticed a rise in recruiting impersonations across the industry, where scammers attempt to access candidates' personal and financial information through fake interviews and offers. All Cresta recruiting email communications will always come from the @cresta.ai domain.&nbsp;Any outreach claiming to be from Cresta via other sources should be ignored.&nbsp; If you are uncertain whether you have been contacted by an official Cresta employee, reach out to <a href="mailto:recruiting@cresra.ai">recruiting@cresta.ai</a></p>

title: Forward Deployed Engineer - Applied AI
location: Gallup, New Mexico, United States; Michigan, United States; Missouri, United States; San Francisco; West Virginia, United States
full_description: <p><strong>About Wonderschool</strong><br>Wonderschool’s mission is to ensure every child has access to early childhood education that helps them realize their full potential. We empower small business owners, childcare providers, to launch and grow in-home programs through cutting-edge software, personalized coaching, and responsive support. We also partner with governments to modernize public childcare infrastructure. Our team is small, fast-moving, and deeply motivated by the impact we create in families and communities across the country.</p> <p><strong>About the Role</strong><br>We are looking for a Forward Deployed AI Engineer to lead product development on some of the most strategically important initiatives at Wonderschool. You’ll spend your time working directly with customers (traveling as needed), uncovering pain points, and building AI-powered solutions that drive measurable impact across revenue, operational efficiency, and user experience. You’ll act like a founder inside the company: identifying problems, shipping code, driving adoption, and iterating quickly based on what you learn. This role blends engineering, product, operations, and business leadership, and is ideal for someone who has launched products, acquired users, and driven growth independently. You will work closely with our sales, onboarding, and account management teams to automate workflows, build better customer experiences, and unlock new growth.</p> <p>This role is inspired by best-in-class FDSEs at companies like Palantir and is perfect for builders who love solving real-world problems in the field, not from behind a desk.</p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do</strong><br>Work directly with users and internal teams to identify high-leverage problems and ship product solutions. Write production code (approximately 50% of your time) and ship weekly improvements across the customer journey. Use tools like Widesurf, Cursor, and other AI accelerators to increase your velocity and quality of output. Build and deploy AI-powered tooling that eliminates manual effort and improves the experience for providers, parents, and government partners. Automate onboarding flows, proposal generation, reporting processes, and other operational workflows. Own full loops: talk to users, design solutions, write code, test, deploy, and monitor adoption. Prioritize based on business impact and collaborate across teams to drive measurable results.</p> <p><strong>What Success Looks Like</strong><br>You uncover user pain points faster than anyone else and consistently ship solutions that address them. You deploy AI-infused internal tools or product experiences that improve core metrics (conversion, retention, revenue). You fully own feedback loops, from discovery to deployment, and iterate rapidly. Internal teams rely on your tools to operate faster and with fewer resources. You’re moving fast, staying close to the customer, and operating with very little hand-holding.</p> <p><strong>Who You Are</strong><br>A builder and founder at heart, you’ve started a company, built products that got users, or made revenue independently. Comfortable talking to users weekly and distilling insight into product decisions. Technical enough to ship production code on your own, experience with Python, JavaScript, or similar languages. Experienced using AI/LLMs and developer tools like Windsurf and Cursor to move faster and smarter. Business-minded, you’ve worked in consulting, managed a P&amp;L, or attended business school and know how to prioritize based on ROI. Comfortable operating in ambiguity and energized by a high-trust, high-impact, in-person environment.</p> <p><strong>Why Join Us</strong><br>Make a direct impact on families, small business owners, and communities. Operate with founder-like ownership on the company’s most important priorities. Join a small, fast-moving team with a bias for action. Talk to users, ship weekly, and see your work go live and drive real-world outcomes. Help reimagine early education and child care for the modern era.</p> <p><strong>Location</strong></p> <p>In-person, 5-6 days a week. This role requires regular client travel.</p>
